Production Frontend Implementation Spec — v6 (Convergence Round 6) ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 0. PRE-MIGRATION AUDIT & BACKEND ENDPOINT MAPPING ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Before any frontend code is written, execute this checklist: Step M0: Feature inventory & branch setup. - Create git branch: `git checkout -b frontend-react-migration` - Run the existing Alpine/Tailwind index.html in a browser. - Screenshot every visible screen state and list all interactive features. - Save screenshots to `_legacy/screenshots/` as the regression checklist. - This 10-minute inventory prevents 10 hours of "didn't the old version do X?" Step 1: Audit web_server.py routes. - List every route handler in web_server.py. - Map each existing endpoint to the api.js fetch function it will serve. - If backend uses /api/v1/search, api.js calls /api/v1/search — the frontend adapts to the backend, never the reverse. - Document the exact mapping in a ROUTES.md file at repo root. Step 2: Confirm static-file serving behavior. - Verify web_server.py serves index.html for GET / with no path-based routing for sub-pages. - Confirm it serves static assets from src/frontend/web/ (CSS, JS, fonts, images). - If web_server.py has catch-all or path-based HTML serving, document it — this affects routing strategy. Step 3: Verify API health endpoint exists. - If GET /api/health (or equivalent) exists, map it. If not, flag for backend team to add. - api.js uses this on Shell mount to display connectivity warnings (non-blocking — see Section 7). Step 4: Runtime 404 detection — distinguish dev errors from data 404s. - api.js must log a clear console.error with the endpoint path when it receives a 404. - API-route 404 message: "Endpoint not found: GET /api/foo — verify backend route mapping in ROUTES.md" - Resource 404 (e.g., /api/docs/:id where :id doesn't exist): render — no retry button. - Dev-error 404 (endpoint itself missing): render — retryable. - Distinguish by convention: if the route pattern matches a known endpoint template but the specific resource ID fails, it's a resource 404. If the route pattern itself is unknown, it's a dev error. Step 5: Verify Content-Type headers. - Confirm web_server.py serves .js files with Content-Type: application/javascript (not text/plain). - Confirm .css files with Content-Type: text/css. - Confirm .woff2 files with Content-Type: font/woff2. - Some Python HTTP servers (http.server, SimpleHTTPServer) serve incorrect MIME types. Browsers refuse ES module execution with strict MIME type errors — this is a silent, hard-to-diagnose failure. - If MIME types are wrong, fix in web_server.py before proceeding. Migration sequence (big-bang replacement — no feature flags): Step M1: Copy current index.html → _legacy/index-alpine.html (preservation, not coexistence). Step M2: Create new index.html with React mount point, theme script, CSS links. index.html includes two root divs:
Step M2.5: Create package.json in src/frontend/web/ with: dependencies: react@18, react-dom@18 devDependencies: esbuild@0.20+, eslint, eslint-plugin-react-hooks, eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y scripts: "build": "esbuild src/main.jsx --bundle --format=esm --outdir=dist --minify --target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90 --metafile=meta.json --jsx=automatic" "dev": "esbuild src/main.jsx --bundle --format=esm --outdir=dist --target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90 --jsx=automatic --sourcemap=inline --watch" "check-budget": "node scripts/check-bundle-size.js" "lint": "eslint src/" All paths in package.json scripts are relative to src/frontend/web/ (where package.json lives). Run npm install. Commit package.json and package-lock.json. Add node_modules/ to .gitignore. Add dist/ to .gitignore. Step M3: Run `cd src/frontend/web && npm run build` → dist/main.js. web_server.py serves new index.html at / unchanged. Step M3.5: Verify MIME types (Step 5 above) by loading dist/main.js in browser — check DevTools Network tab for Content-Type and console for MIME errors. Step M4: Smoke-test all API routes from new frontend against running backend. Step M4.5: Visual regression — screenshot all 3 key screens and compare against _legacy/screenshots/. Step M5: Remove _legacy/ after 2 weeks of production stability. Rollback: revert index.html to _legacy/index-alpine.html. Zero backend changes required. Dev workflow (exact terminal commands): Terminal 1: cd src/frontend/web && npm run dev Terminal 2: python src/backend/apps/web_server.py esbuild watch rebuilds on file change. web_server.py serves the whole web/ directory. API requests go to web_server.py directly (same origin, no CORS). No proxy needed. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 1. BUILD SYSTEM & MODULE STRATEGY ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Build tool: esbuild (single dependency, zero-config). Decision: esbuild bundles React into the output. No vendor/ directory. No import maps. Rationale: import maps require Safari 16.4+ / Firefox 108+ minimum. Bundling React eliminates the compatibility risk entirely and simplifies the build to a single command. The bundle size difference is zero since React ends up in the output either way. Production command (run from src/frontend/web/): npx esbuild src/main.jsx \ --bundle \ --format=esm \ --outdir=dist \ --minify \ --target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90 \ --metafile=meta.json \ --jsx=automatic Development command (run from src/frontend/web/): npx esbuild src/main.jsx \ --bundle \ --format=esm \ --outdir=dist \ --target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90 \ --jsx=automatic \ --sourcemap=inline \ --watch NOTE: If running from repo root, prefix all commands with `cd src/frontend/web &&`. All paths in this spec are relative to src/frontend/web/ unless explicitly stated otherwise. --jsx=automatic rationale: enables React 18's automatic JSX runtime. Components do NOT need `import React from 'react'` at the top. esbuild injects jsx-runtime imports automatically. Without this flag, any component missing the React import silently fails at runtime. Target rationale: using explicit browser targets instead of es2020 lets esbuild make optimal per-browser syntax decisions. This is self-documenting of the actual support matrix. IMPORTANT: esbuild --target downlevels SYNTAX only, not runtime APIs. Banned APIs (not available on browser floor): - AbortController.timeout() — Safari 16+ only - Array.prototype.at() — Safari 15.4+ only (borderline, avoid) - Object.hasOwn() — Safari 15.4+ only - structuredClone() — Safari 15.4+ only - crypto.randomUUID() — Safari 15.4+ only Enforcement: Add ESLint no-restricted-properties / no-restricted-globals rules: "no-restricted-properties": ["error", { "object": "Object", "property": "hasOwn", "message": "Use Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call() — Object.hasOwn unsupported on Safari <15.4" }, { "property": "at", "message": "Use bracket notation — Array.at() unsupported on Safari <15.4" } ], "no-restricted-globals": ["error", { "name": "structuredClone", "message": "Use JSON parse/stringify or manual clone — structuredClone unsupported on Safari <15.4" } ] CSS strategy: tokens.css and animations.css are NOT imported by JS. They are standalone references in index.html, loaded directly by the browser. esbuild does not process them. This is intentional — global design tokens and keyframes are authored CSS, not component CSS. No --splitting in v1 (splitting requires import maps for shared chunks in browsers; we just eliminated import maps). Single entry bundle. Code-split in v2 via dynamic import() if bundle exceeds budget. Bundle budget enforcement: CI step: `npm run lint && npm run build && npm run check-budget` Budget: dist/main.js gzipped ≤ 80KB. Budget breakdown (estimated): React 18 + ReactDOM: ~42KB gzipped App components: ~15KB gzipped Icons (tree-shaken): ~3KB gzipped Total JS: ~60KB gzipped (20KB headroom to 80KB cap) Separate from JS budget: CSS (tokens + anim): ~4KB gzipped (loaded via , not in JS bundle) Fonts (subsetted): ≤25KB gzipped total (see font budget below) Total transfer: ~89KB gzipped first load scripts/check-bundle-size.js implementation: const fs = require('fs'); const zlib = require('zlib'); const meta = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('meta.json', 'utf8')); const BUDGET = 80 * 1024; // 80KB let totalGzip = 0; for (const [file, info] of Object.entries(meta.outputs)) { if (file.endsWith('.js')) { const raw = fs.readFileSync(file); const gzipped = zlib.gzipSync(raw); console.log(` ${file}: ${(raw.length / 1024).toFixed(1)}KB raw, ${(gzipped.length / 1024).toFixed(1)}KB gzip`); totalGzip += gzipped.length; } } console.log(`\nTotal JS gzipped: ${(totalGzip / 1024).toFixed(1)}KB / ${(BUDGET / 1024).toFixed(0)}KB budget`); if (totalGzip > BUDGET) { console.error('BUDGET EXCEEDED'); process.exit(1); } Preact escape hatch: if bundle exceeds 80KB, switch via esbuild alias: --alias:react=preact/compat --alias:react-dom=preact/compat Preact compat is ~4KB vs React's 42KB. Document this as v1.1 option, not default. Font budget: Inter in 2 weights for v1: Regular (400) and Semibold (600). Medium (500) dropped. Subset to Basic Latin + Latin-1 Supplement + common punctuation: U+0000-00FF, U+2010-2027. Subsetting command: pyftsubset inter-regular.woff2 --unicodes='U+0000-00FF,U+2010-2027' --flavor=woff2 Target per-weight: ≤15KB uncompressed, ≤7KB gzipped. Total font budget: ≤25KB gzipped for both weights. If subsetting tooling is unavailable, document as pre-launch blocker. Medium weight faked via: font-weight: 500 with font-synthesis: weight (browser synthesizes). v1.1: add Medium weight file if synthesized weight is visually unacceptable. Linting (non-negotiable for v1): devDependencies: eslint, eslint-plugin-react-hooks, eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y .eslintrc.json: { "plugins": ["react-hooks", "jsx-a11y"], "extends": ["plugin:jsx-a11y/recommended"], "rules": { "react-hooks/rules-of-hooks": "error", "react-hooks/exhaustive-deps": "warn", "no-restricted-properties": ["error", ...banned API rules above] } } CI runs `npm run lint` before build. This catches stale closures, missing dependencies, and accessibility violations at build time. Core Web Vitals targets (non-negotiable): LCP: < 2.5s on 3G throttled FID / INP: < 100ms CLS: < 0.1 Implementation requirements to hit these: - index.html inlines critical CSS for above-fold content (shell chrome, skeleton placeholders). - tokens.css and animations.css loaded via (not @import — avoids waterfall). - Fonts loaded via with font-display: swap. - All above-fold layout uses explicit dimensions (no layout shift from late-loading content). - Skeleton placeholders match final content dimensions to prevent CLS on data arrival. - Main content area uses persistent padding-bottom for tab bar space (see Section 3 — CLS prevention). Source code lives in src/frontend/web/src/ (JSX, full React idioms). Built output lands in src/frontend/web/dist/ (static ES module, minified, tree-shaken). index.html references dist/main.js via

@@ -1,29 +1,525 @@
-Production frontend implementation spec to be approved before code changes.
-
-Repository constraints:
-- Serve from src/frontend/web through src/backend/apps/web_server.py
-- Preserve current backend endpoints and direct static serving
-- Replace current Alpine-based index with a React production shell after approval
-
-Implementation direction:
-1. Use React 18 functional components with hooks, loaded as production ES modules from CDN/import map, with no runtime JSX compiler and no bundler requirement. All app code lives in static JS modules under src/frontend/web/app/.
-2. File layout:
-   - src/frontend/web/index.html
-   - src/frontend/web/tokens.css
-   - src/frontend/web/app/main.js
-   - src/frontend/web/app/state.js
-   - src/frontend/web/app/api.js
-   - src/frontend/web/app/icons.js
-   - src/frontend/web/app/components/{shell,tabbar,bottom-sheet,skeleton,status-card,timeline-item,search-composer,empty-state}.js
-   - src/frontend/web/app/screens/{home,detail,action-sheet}.js
-3. Navigation: bottom tab bar with max 4 tabs (Home, Timeline, Search, Actions). Primary action always reachable in one tap; secondary actions live in bottom sheets only.
-4. Home/Dashboard: first paint shows header, primary KPI rail, live status card, and skeleton placeholders. Progressive cascade loads top searches, examples, recent results, and system health snippets.
-5. Detail/Timeline: chronological transaction-style timeline around the selected document/search journey, with color-coded operational states, sticky summary header, and contextual quick actions.
-6. Action sheet/Form: bottom-sheet flow for primary actions such as filtered search presets and document actions; no full-page simple forms.
-7. Design tokens: dark-first CSS variables for surfaces, text, accent, status, spacing, type scale, shadows, radii, motion curves, and z-index layers. Light theme is variable override only.
-8. Motion: transform/opacity-only classes for spring-in, float, breathe, sway, sheet-lift, badge-pulse; reduced-motion variants disable non-essential motion.
-9. Interaction patterns: swipe affordance on result/timeline items on touch devices, long-press quick actions, pull-to-refresh on data screens where supported, optimistic state updates for local UI actions.
-10. Accessibility: 44x44 minimum hit targets, WCAG AA contrast, semantic landmarks, focus-visible states, keyboard sheet dismissal/focus trap, reduced motion, aria-live for status updates.
-11. Performance: no spinners, skeleton-only loading, lazy mount lower-priority panes, request cancellation/debounce for autocomplete, split static JS modules by feature, avoid heavy runtime dependencies.
-12. Migration path: keep existing backend unchanged, add new static assets, switch index.html to React shell, retain legacy artifacts until parity check passes, then quarantine obsolete frontend files.
-13. Acceptance: concrete component map, token map, screen behaviors, data flow, and implementation file plan that all four reviewers approve before coding.
+Production Frontend Implementation Spec — v2 (Revised for Convergence)
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+1. BUILD SYSTEM & MODULE STRATEGY
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+Build tool: esbuild (single dependency, zero-config).
+Command: esbuild src/frontend/web/src/main.jsx --bundle --splitting --format=esm --outdir=src/frontend/web/dist --minify --target=es2020
+Source code lives in src/frontend/web/src/ (JSX, full React idioms).
+Built output lands in src/frontend/web/dist/ (static ES modules, minified, tree-shaken, code-split).
+index.html references dist/main.js via <script type="module">.
+No dev server. No HMR. No webpack. One deterministic build command.
+React 18 and ReactDOM self-hosted in src/frontend/web/vendor/ (no CDN dependency).
+Import map in index.html resolves "react" and "react-dom" to local vendor paths.
+web_server.py serves src/frontend/web/ unchanged — only static file additions.
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+2. FILE LAYOUT
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+src/frontend/web/
+├── index.html                          # Shell HTML, import map, theme-init script
+├── tokens.css                          # Design tokens (dark default + light override)
+├── animations.css                      # Keyframes + motion utility classes
+├── vendor/
+│   ├── react.production.min.js         # Self-hosted React 18
+│   └── react-dom.production.min.js     # Self-hosted ReactDOM 18
+├── src/                                # SOURCE (JSX, authored code)
+│   ├── main.jsx                        # Entry: mounts <App/>
+│   ├── app.jsx                         # App root: ThemeProvider → Router → Shell
+│   ├── state/
+│   │   ├── theme.jsx                   # ThemeContext + useTheme hook
+│   │   ├── navigation.jsx              # RouterContext + useRoute, History API
+│   │   └── data.jsx                    # DataContext + useReducer, fetch cache
+│   ├── api.js                          # All fetch functions, AbortController, error handling
+│   ├── utils.js                        # cx() class merger, debounce, format helpers
+│   ├── icons/                          # One file per icon, Phosphor-style, 1.5px stroke
+│   │   ├── home.jsx
+│   │   ├── search.jsx
+│   │   ├── library.jsx
+│   │   ├── chevron-left.jsx
+│   │   ├── more-horizontal.jsx
+│   │   ├── status-dot.jsx
+│   │   ├── alert-circle.jsx
+│   │   └── index.js                    # Barrel re-export (tree-shaken by esbuild)
+│   ├── components/
+│   │   ├── shell.jsx                   # Main layout: header + content + tab bar
+│   │   ├── tab-bar.jsx                 # 3-tab bottom nav
+│   │   ├── bottom-sheet.jsx            # Draggable sheet w/ focus trap + portal
+│   │   ├── focus-trap.jsx              # Generic focus trap utility
+│   │   ├── portal.jsx                  # React portal to document.body
+│   │   ├── skeleton.jsx                # High-fidelity skeleton variants
+│   │   ├── status-card.jsx             # Live operational status
+│   │   ├── kpi-metric.jsx              # Single KPI display
+│   │   ├── timeline-item.jsx           # Single timeline row
+│   │   ├── search-composer.jsx         # Search input + preset chips
+│   │   ├── empty-state.jsx             # Empty/zero-data illustration
+│   │   ├── error-state.jsx             # API failure w/ retry action
+│   │   ├── error-boundary.jsx          # React error boundary (catches render crashes)
+│   │   └── route-announcer.jsx         # aria-live region for SPA navigation
+│   └── screens/
+│       ├── home.jsx                    # Dashboard / Home tab
+│       ├── search.jsx                  # Search tab
+│       ├── library.jsx                 # Library/History tab
+│       └── detail.jsx                  # Drill-down timeline view (not a tab)
+├── dist/                               # BUILD OUTPUT (generated, gitignored option)
+│   ├── main.js
+│   └── [chunk-*].js
+└── _legacy/                            # Quarantined old Alpine files (post-migration)
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+3. NAVIGATION & INFORMATION ARCHITECTURE
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+Bottom tab bar: 3 tabs (Home, Search, Library).
+  - Tabs are parallel peer destinations. No drill-down views in tabs.
+  - Active tab indicated by accent fill + label; inactive tabs show outline icon only.
+  - Tab bar hidden when bottom sheet is open (avoids thumb zone congestion).
+
+Drill-down views (Detail/Timeline): accessed from tapping items on Home or Search results.
+  - Back affordance: chevron-left button in top-left header area.
+  - Push onto a navigation stack managed by History API.
+
+Routing strategy: hash-based (#/) for zero-config with web_server.py static serving.
+  Routes:
+    #/              → Home screen
+    #/search        → Search screen
+    #/library       → Library screen
+    #/doc/:id       → Detail/Timeline screen (drill-down, not a tab)
+
+  state/navigation.jsx exports:
+    RouterContext: { route: string, params: Record<string,string>, push(path), back() }
+    useRoute(): reads context
+    <Router>: listens to hashchange, updates context, announces to route-announcer
+
+  Focus management: on route change, move focus to <main> heading (h1) and announce via route-announcer aria-live region.
+
+Bottom sheet: used for primary action flows (new search presets, document actions).
+  - Invoked from contextual buttons (not a tab destination).
+  - Opens over content, tab bar hides behind overlay.
+  - Dismiss: drag handle down, Escape key, overlay tap.
+  - Focus trapped inside while open.
+
+Quick actions on list items: explicit "⋯" (more) icon button (44×44 tap target). No long-press. No swipe gestures in v1.
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+4. COMPONENT ARCHITECTURE MAP
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+<App>
+├── <ThemeProvider value={{ theme, toggle }}>
+│   ├── <Router>
+│   │   ├── <RouteAnnouncer />                    // aria-live="assertive" for route changes
+│   │   ├── <ErrorBoundary fallback={<ErrorState />}>
+│   │   │   ├── <Shell>
+│   │   │   │   ├── <header>                       // Sticky top bar, title + optional back button
+│   │   │   │   ├── <main role="main">             // Screen content swaps here
+│   │   │   │   │   ├── route #/ →
+│   │   │   │   │   │   <HomeScreen>
+│   │   │   │   │   │   ├── <KPIRail>
+│   │   │   │   │   │   │   └── <KPIMetric label:string value:string trend:"up"|"down"|"flat" /> ×3 max
+│   │   │   │   │   │   ├── <StatusCard status:Status lastUpdated:Date />
+│   │   │   │   │   │   ├── <SearchPresets presets:Preset[] onSelect:(p)=>void />
+│   │   │   │   │   │   └── <RecentResults items:Result[] /> | <EmptyState /> | <ErrorState onRetry />
+│   │   │   │   │   │
+│   │   │   │   │   ├── route #/search →
+│   │   │   │   │   │   <SearchScreen>
+│   │   │   │   │   │   ├── <SearchComposer query:string onChange onSubmit />
+│   │   │   │   │   │   └── <ResultsList items:Result[] /> | <Skeleton variant="list" /> | <ErrorState />
+│   │   │   │   │   │
+│   │   │   │   │   ├── route #/library →
+│   │   │   │   │   │   <LibraryScreen>
+│   │   │   │   │   │   └── <HistoryList items:HistoryEntry[] /> | <EmptyState />
+│   │   │   │   │   │
+│   │   │   │   │   └── route #/doc/:id →
+│   │   │   │   │       <DetailScreen id:string>
+│   │   │   │   │       ├── <StickyHeader title:string status:StatusBadge />
+│   │   │   │   │       ├── <Timeline entries:TimelineEntry[] />
+│   │   │   │   │       │   └── <TimelineItem timestamp:Date label:string state:"success"|"warning"|"error"|"info"|"pending" detail?:string /> ×N
+│   │   │   │   │       └── <QuickActions actions:Action[] onAction:(a)=>void />  // "⋯" menu triggers bottom sheet
+│   │   │   │   │
+│   │   │   │   └── <TabBar activeTab:string onNavigate:(tab)=>void />
+│   │   │   │
+│   │   │   └── <Portal>
+│   │   │       └── <BottomSheet open:boolean onClose title:string>
+│   │   │           └── <FocusTrap> {children} </FocusTrap>
+│   │   │       </BottomSheet>
+│   │   │   </Portal>
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+5. DATA TYPES & API CONTRACT
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+// api.js — all functions accept AbortSignal, return typed promises, throw on non-2xx
+
+type Metric = { id: string, label: string, value: string, trend: "up"|"down"|"flat", delta?: string }
+type Status = { state: "operational"|"degraded"|"down", message: string, lastUpdated: string /*ISO*/ }
+type Preset = { id: string, label: string, query: string, icon?: string }
+type Result = { id: string, title: string, snippet: string, timestamp: string, status: string }
+type HistoryEntry = { id: string, query: string, resultCount: number, timestamp: string }
+type TimelineEntry = { id: string, timestamp: string, label: string, state: "success"|"warning"|"error"|"info"|"pending", detail?: string }
+type Action = { id: string, label: string, icon: string, destructive?: boolean }
+
+fetchMetrics(signal?): Promise<Metric[]>          // GET /api/metrics
+fetchStatus(signal?): Promise<Status>              // GET /api/status
+fetchPresets(signal?): Promise<Preset[]>           // GET /api/presets
+searchDocuments(query, signal?): Promise<Result[]> // GET /api/search?q=
+fetchHistory(signal?): Promise<HistoryEntry[]>     // GET /api/history
+fetchTimeline(docId, signal?): Promise<TimelineEntry[]>  // GET /api/docs/:id/timeline
+fetchActions(docId, signal?): Promise<Action[]>    // GET /api/docs/:id/actions
+executeAction(docId, actionId, signal?): Promise<void>   // POST /api/docs/:id/actions/:actionId
+
+Error handling:
+- All fetchers throw ApiError({ status, message, retryable }) on failure.
+- 8-second timeout via AbortController.timeout(8000) — on timeout, throw with retryable: true.
+- Callers catch and render <ErrorState message onRetry /> or <ErrorState message /> (non-retryable).
+
+Autocomplete debounce: 200ms in SearchComposer. Previous in-flight request cancelled via AbortController before new request fires.
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+6. STATE MANAGEMENT
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+Global state via React Context + useReducer (no external dependencies):
+
+ThemeContext:
+  State: { mode: "dark"|"light" }
+  Actions: TOGGLE_THEME
+  Persistence: localStorage key "app-theme", fallback to prefers-color-scheme
+
+RouterContext:
+  State: { path: string, params: Record<string,string>, stack: string[] }
+  Actions: PUSH, BACK, REPLACE
+  Sync: hashchange listener in <Router> dispatches updates
+
+DataContext (optional — only if cross-screen data sharing needed):
+  State: { metrics: Metric[]|null, status: Status|null, cache: Map<string, {data, timestamp}> }
+  Stale-while-revalidate: serve cached data immediately, refetch if cache age > 30s.
+  No external library (SWR/React Query). Minimal custom hook: useFetch(key, fetcher, { staleTime }).
+
+Component-local state via useState/useReducer for:
+  - Search input value
+  - Bottom sheet open/closed
+  - Skeleton/loaded/error loading phases
+  - Form field values in action sheets
+
+Prop drilling banned past 2 levels. Use context or component composition (render props / children) instead.
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+7. DESIGN TOKEN SYSTEM (tokens.css)
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+/* ── Typography ── */
+Font stack: 'Inter', 'SF Pro Display', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif
+Font loading: Inter self-hosted as woff2 in src/frontend/web/fonts/inter-{regular,medium,semibold}.woff2
+  - <link rel="preload" as="font" type="font/woff2" crossorigin> in <head>
+  - font-display: swap
+
+:root {
+  /* ── Surfaces (dark default) ── */
+  --surface-0: #09090b;           /* App background — zinc-950 */
+  --surface-1: #18181b;           /* Card background — zinc-900 */
+  --surface-2: #27272a;           /* Elevated card / input — zinc-800 */
+  --surface-3: #3f3f46;           /* Hover / pressed — zinc-700 */
+
+  /* ── Text ── */
+  --text-primary: #fafafa;        /* zinc-50 */
+  --text-secondary: #a1a1aa;      /* zinc-400 */
+  --text-tertiary: #71717a;       /* zinc-500 */
+  --text-inverse: #09090b;        /* For use on accent backgrounds */
+
+  /* ── Brand / Accent ── */
+  --accent-default: #a78bfa;      /* violet-400 — confident, premium */
+  --accent-hover: #c4b5fd;        /* violet-300 */
+  --accent-active: #8b5cf6;       /* violet-500 */
+  --accent-subtle: rgba(167, 139, 250, 0.12);
+
+  /* ── Status ── */
+  --status-success: #34d399;      /* emerald-400 */
+  --status-warning: #fbbf24;      /* amber-400 */
+  --status-error: #f87171;        /* red-400 */
+  --status-info: #60a5fa;         /* blue-400 */
+  --status-pending: #a1a1aa;      /* zinc-400 */
+
+  /* ── Borders ── */
+  --border-default: #27272a;      /* zinc-800 */
+  --border-subtle: #1e1e22;
+  --border-accent: var(--accent-default);
+
+  /* ── Spacing (4px base) ── */
+  --space-1: 4px;
+  --space-2: 8px;
+  --space-3: 12px;
+  --space-4: 16px;
+  --space-5: 20px;
+  --space-6: 24px;
+  --space-8: 32px;
+  --space-10: 40px;
+  --space-12: 48px;
+  --space-16: 64px;
+
+  /* ── Typography Scale ── */
+  --font-xs: 0.75rem;     /* 12px, line-height 1rem */
+  --font-sm: 0.875rem;    /* 14px, line-height 1.25rem */
+  --font-base: 1rem;      /* 16px, line-height 1.5rem */
+  --font-lg: 1.125rem;    /* 18px, line-height 1.75rem */
+  --font-xl: 1.25rem;     /* 20px, line-height 1.75rem */
+  --font-2xl: 1.5rem;     /* 24px, line-height 2rem */
+  --font-3xl: 1.875rem;   /* 30px, line-height 2.25rem */
+  --lh-tight: 1.25;
+  --lh-normal: 1.5;
+  --lh-relaxed: 1.625;
+
+  /* ── Font Weights ── */
+  --weight-regular: 400;
+  --weight-medium: 500;
+  --weight-semibold: 600;
+
+  /* ── Radii ── */
+  --radius-sm: 6px;
+  --radius-md: 10px;
+  --radius-lg: 16px;
+  --radius-full: 9999px;
+
+  /* ── Shadows (subtle, no heavy elevation) ── */
+  --shadow-sm: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
+  --shadow-md: 0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.4);
+  --shadow-lg: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);
+  --shadow-sheet: 0 -4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);
+
+  /* ── Z-index Layers ── */
+  --z-base: 0;
+  --z-sticky: 100;
+  --z-dropdown: 200;
+  --z-overlay: 300;
+  --z-sheet: 400;
+  --z-toast: 500;
+
+  /* ── Motion ── */
+  --ease-expo-out: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
+  --ease-spring: cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1);
+  --ease-smooth: cubic-bezier(0.33, 1, 0.68, 1);
+  --ease-snap: cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1);
+  --duration-instant: 100ms;
+  --duration-fast: 150ms;
+  --duration-normal: 300ms;
+  --duration-slow: 450ms;
+
+  /* ── Breakpoints (reference, used in media queries) ── */
+  /* sm: 375px (base), md: 768px, lg: 1440px */
+}
+
+[data-theme="light"] {
+  --surface-0: #ffffff;
+  --surface-1: #f4f4f5;           /* zinc-100 */
+  --surface-2: #e4e4e7;           /* zinc-200 */
+  --surface-3: #d4d4d8;           /* zinc-300 */
+  --text-primary: #09090b;
+  --text-secondary: #52525b;      /* zinc-600 */
+  --text-tertiary: #71717a;       /* zinc-500 */
+  --text-inverse: #fafafa;
+  --accent-default: #7c3aed;      /* violet-600 — darker for light bg contrast */
+  --accent-hover: #6d28d9;        /* violet-700 */
+  --accent-active: #8b5cf6;       /* violet-500 */
+  --accent-subtle: rgba(124, 58, 237, 0.08);
+  --border-default: #e4e4e7;
+  --border-subtle: #f4f4f5;
+  --shadow-sm: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.05);
+  --shadow-md: 0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.08);
+  --shadow-lg: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.12);
+  --shadow-sheet: 0 -4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.12);
+}
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+8. RESPONSIVE STRATEGY
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+Approach: Tailwind responsive prefixes (sm:, md:, lg:) mapped to breakpoints.
+  - Base (375px+): mobile-first, single column, bottom tab bar.
+  - md (768px+): 2-column on Home (KPI rail + status side-by-side). Tab bar remains bottom.
+  - lg (1440px+): sidebar nav replaces bottom tab bar. 3-column layout. Max-width 1280px centered.
+
+Fluid typography via clamp():
+  --font-display: clamp(1.5rem, 1.2rem + 1vw, 1.875rem);  /* Hero/page titles */
+  --font-body: clamp(0.875rem, 0.825rem + 0.25vw, 1rem);   /* Body text */
+
+No container queries in v1 (insufficient Safari support for production). Media queries only.
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+9. MOTION SYSTEM (animations.css)
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+All animations use transform + opacity only (GPU-composited, no layout triggers).
+All durations reference CSS custom properties.
+Aesthetic: Nubank/Flighty — quick, confident, minimal overshoot. No playful/organic motion.
+
+/* ── Entrance: snap-in ── */
+.motion-snap-in {
+  animation: snap-in var(--duration-normal) var(--ease-spring) both;
+}
+@keyframes snap-in {
+  from { opacity: 0; transform: scale(0.95); }
+  to   { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); }
+}
+
+/* ── Transition: glide ── */
+.motion-glide {
+  animation: glide var(--duration-normal) var(--ease-expo-out) both;
+}
+@keyframes glide {
+  from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(8px); }
+  to   { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }
+}
+
+/* ── Expand: reveal (for bottom sheet) ── */
+.motion-reveal {
+  animation: reveal var(--duration-slow) var(--ease-expo-out) both;
+}
+@keyframes reveal {
+  from { transform: translateY(100%); }
+  to   { transform: translateY(0); }
+}
+
+/* ── Exit: dismiss ── */
+.motion-dismiss {
+  animation: dismiss var(--duration-normal) var(--ease-snap) both;
+}
+@keyframes dismiss {
+  from { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }
+  to   { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(16px); }
+}
+
+/* ── Status: pulse (badge/dot, subtle) ── */
+.motion-pulse {
+  animation: pulse 2s var(--ease-smooth) infinite;
+}
+@keyframes pulse {
+  0%, 100% { opacity: 1; }
+  50%      { opacity: 0.6; }
+}
+
+/* ── Stagger utility ── */
+.motion-stagger-1 { animation-delay: 50ms; }
+.motion-stagger-2 { animation-delay: 100ms; }
+.motion-stagger-3 { animation-delay: 150ms; }
+.motion-stagger-4 { animation-delay: 200ms; }
+
+/* ── Skeleton shimmer ── */
+.skeleton-shimmer {
+  background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--surface-1) 0%, var(--surface-2) 50%, var(--surface-1) 100%);
+  background-size: 200% 100%;
+  animation: shimmer 1.5s var(--ease-smooth) infinite;
+}
+@keyframes shimmer {
+  from { background-position: 200% 0; }
+  to   { background-position: -200% 0; }
+}
+
+/* ── Reduced motion ── */
+@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
+  *, *::before, *::after {
+    animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
+    animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
+    transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
+  }
+}
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+10. ICON SYSTEM
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+Style: Phosphor-inspired custom SVG icons.
+Stroke: 1.5px uniform.
+Sizes: 20px (tab bar, inline), 24px (header actions), 32px (empty states).
+Color: currentColor (inherits text color from parent).
+Each icon is a single JSX component returning <svg> with viewBox="0 0 24 24".
+Barrel export from icons/index.js — esbuild tree-shakes unused icons.
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+11. SCREEN SPECIFICATIONS
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+─── HOME SCREEN (#/) ───
+
+Loading phases:
+  Phase 1 (immediate, 0ms): Shell renders with header ("Dashboard"), KPI rail skeleton (3 cards: 80×48px rounded-md), status card skeleton (full-width 96px rounded-lg).
+  Phase 2 (parallel fetch): fetchMetrics() + fetchStatus() fire simultaneously.
+  Phase 3 (on resolve): skeleton → content with .motion-glide + stagger. KPI rail max 3 metrics.
+  Phase 4 (deferred, after Phase 3 paint): fetchPresets(), fetchHistory() for lower sections.
+  Error: if Phase 2 fails after 8s timeout → replace skeleton with <ErrorState message="Unable to load dashboard" onRetry />.
+
+Skeleton fidelity: skeleton components match exact dimensions of resolved content (KPI card skeleton = same height/width/border-radius as KPIMetric). No generic gray bars.
+
+Content reveal: .motion-glide with .motion-stagger-{1,2,3} on sequential cards.
+
+StatusCard: live-updates via polling (30s interval) with aria-live="polite" region. State renders colored dot (--status-*) + text.
+
+─── SEARCH SCREEN (#/search) ───
+
+SearchComposer: sticky top, input with 200ms debounce, AbortController cancels previous in-flight. Preset chips below input.
+Results: list of Result cards, each with title, snippet, timestamp, status badge. Tap navigates to #/doc/:id.
+Loading: skeleton list (5 items, each 72px height matching Result card).
+Empty: <EmptyState message="No results found" />.
+Error: <ErrorState onRetry />.
+
+─── LIBRARY SCREEN (#/library) ───
+
+Chronological list of past searches/interactions.
+Loading: skeleton list.
+Empty: <EmptyState message="No history yet" />.
+
+─── DETAIL SCREEN (#/doc/:id) ───
+
+Header: sticky, shows document title + status badge. Back chevron top-left.
+Timeline: vertical line with TimelineItem nodes. Each node: colored dot (state-based), timestamp, label, optional detail expansion.
+Quick actions: "⋯" button in header triggers <BottomSheet> with action list.
+Loading: skeleton with sticky header placeholder + 5 timeline item skeletons.
+Error: <ErrorState onRetry />.
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+12. ACCESSIBILITY
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+WCAG 2.1 AA compliance required.
+
+Hit targets: minimum 44×44px on all interactive elements.
+Contrast: all text/bg combinations verified against AA ratio (4.5:1 normal, 3:1 large).
+  - Dark: --text-primary (#fafafa) on --surface-0 (#09090b) = 19.4:1 ✓
+  - Dark: --text-secondary (#a1a1aa) on --surface-0 (#09090b) = 7.2:1 ✓
+  - Light: --text-primary (#09090b) on --surface-0 (#ffffff) = 19.4:1 ✓
+  - Light: --accent-default (#7c3aed) on --surface-0 (#ffffff) = 5.4:1 ✓
+
+Semantic HTML: <header>, <main role="main">, <nav> for tab bar, <section> for content regions, <h1>-<h3> hierarchy per screen.
+Skip link: "Skip to content" link as first focusable element, visually hidden until focused, targets <main>.
+Focus-visible: 2px solid var(--accent-default) outline with 2px offset on all focusable elements. No outline removal.
+Focus trap: bottom sheet traps focus. Escape dismisses. Focus returns to trigger element on close.
+Route announcer: <div aria-live="assertive" class="sr-only"> announces "Navigated to {page name}" on route change.
+Status updates: aria-live="polite" on StatusCard, search result count announcements.
+Form errors: aria-live="assertive" + role="alert" on validation error containers in bottom sheet forms.
+Reduced motion: all animation disabled via prefers-reduced-motion (see motion system).
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+13. PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+No spinners anywhere. Skeleton → content → error is the only loading pattern.
+Skeleton display cap: 8 seconds, then transition to error state with retry.
+Lazy mount: Library screen and its data fetch only execute on first tab activation.
+Request cancellation: all fetchers accept AbortSignal. SearchComposer cancels on new keystroke.
+Debounce: 200ms on search input.
+Code splitting: esbuild --splitting produces shared chunks; screens are dynamic import() where beneficial.
+Font loading: preloaded woff2, font-display: swap, FOUT over FOIT.
+Initial bundle target: <60KB gzipped (React + app code).
+No inline styles for dynamic values — CSS custom properties only (preserves theme switching).
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+14. THEME SWITCHING & FOUC PREVENTION
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+Critical-path theme script in <head> BEFORE any CSS or React loads:
+
+<script>
+  (function(){
+    var t = localStorage.getItem('app-theme');
+    if (!t) t = matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme:light)').matches ? 'light' :

objections.description: "React 18 functional components with hooks, loaded as production ES modules from CDN/import map, with no runtime JSX compiler and no bundler requirement" is architecturally contradictory. React without JSX means either htm tagged templates or raw createElement calls across every component. htm adds a runtime template parser (perf tax on every render), and raw createElement is unmaintainable at the component count described (shell, tabbar, bottom-sheet, skeleton, status-card, timeline-item, search-composer, empty-state, 3 screens, state, icons). The spec must pick ONE concrete approach and defend it: (a) htm + preact (smaller, actually designed for no-build), (b) a single-pass esbuild/vite build step that outputs static ES modules (still no dev server required, just a build command), or (c) raw createElement with a thin h() helper. My recommendation: option (b) with esbuild --bundle --splitting --format=esm producing static outputs into src/frontend/web/dist/. This keeps the no-runtime-compiler guarantee, gives you JSX, tree-shakes dead code, and the output is still static files served by web_server.py unchanged.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a minimal build step: `esbuild src/frontend/web/src/main.jsx --bundle --splitting --format=esm --outdir=src/frontend/web/dist --minify`. Source lives in src/frontend/web/src/, built output in src/frontend/web/dist/. index.html references dist/main.js. No dev server, no HMR, no webpack. One command, deterministic output, static serving preserved.

objections.description: "dark-first CSS variables" is stated but no actual token map is provided. This is a spec that must be approved before coding — shipping without a concrete token table means each reviewer is approving a different mental model. We need the actual variable names, their dark values, their light overrides, the type scale steps, spacing scale, and motion curve definitions. Without this, the visual design critic (Qwen) and the frontend engineer (ZAI) cannot meaningfully approve.
objections.suggested_fix: Include a concrete tokens.css draft with at minimum: --surface-{0,1,2,3}, --text-{primary,secondary,tertiary,inverse}, --accent-{default,hover,active}, --status-{success,warning,error,info}, --radius-{sm,md,lg,full}, --shadow-{sm,md,lg}, --space-{1..12} mapped to a 4px base, --font-{xs,sm,base,lg,xl,2xl} with line-heights, --motion-{spring,ease-out-expo,duration-fast,duration-normal}, --z-{sheet,overlay,sticky,dropdown}. Provide both :root (dark) and [data-theme='light'] override blocks.

objections.description: The spec describes screens narratively but provides zero concrete component hierarchy, props contracts, or data flow. 'Primary KPI rail' — what data? From which endpoint? 'Chronological transaction-style timeline' — what's the data shape? What's the API contract? Without a component tree showing parent→child relationships, prop interfaces, and which backend endpoints feed which screens, this is a mood board, not an implementation spec. Four reviewers cannot converge on vibes.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a component architecture map as a concrete tree: App → ThemeProvider → Shell → [TabBar, Screen]. Each Screen lists its child components, their props (with TypeScript-style annotations even if not using TS), and the API endpoint + response shape they consume. Example: HomeScreen → [Header({title:string}), KPIRail({metrics:Metric[]}), StatusCard({status:Status, lastUpdated:Date}), SearchPresets({presets:Preset[]})]. Include api.js function signatures: fetchMetrics():Promise<Metric[]>, fetchTimeline(id:string):Promise<TimelineEntry[]>, etc.

objections.description: Swipe affordance, long-press, and pull-to-refresh on a no-build vanilla JS React app with no gesture library is a significant implementation commitment. The spec doesn't address which gesture library (if any) or whether these are custom pointer event handlers. Custom touch gesture code is a bug factory — especially swipe-to-dismiss with momentum and pull-to-refresh with overscroll containment. This needs scoping: which gestures ship in v1 vs. which are deferred.
objections.suggested_fix: Tier the interaction patterns. v1: tap, keyboard, focus management, bottom sheet drag handle only. v2: swipe affordance on list items, pull-to-refresh. Drop long-press entirely — it has zero discoverability and conflicts with native context menus. If swipe ships in v1, specify @use-gesture/react or a concrete custom implementation approach with touch-action CSS values.

objections.description: Migration path says 'retain legacy artifacts until parity check passes' but doesn't define parity. What's the checklist? Which legacy features must work before the old index.html is removed? Without this, you'll have two frontends in the repo indefinitely — I've seen this movie, it ends with both rotting.
objections.suggested_fix: Define explicit parity checklist: (1) all backend endpoints consumed by old UI are consumed by new UI, (2) all user-facing actions reproducible, (3) lighthouse performance score >= old UI, (4) manual smoke test on iOS Safari, Chrome Android, Chrome Desktop, Firefox Desktop. Set a deadline: legacy files quarantined to src/frontend/web/_legacy/ within 2 weeks of new shell merge, deleted within 4 weeks.

objections.description: Motion system lists class names (spring-in, float, breathe, sway, sheet-lift, badge-pulse) but provides no actual keyframe definitions, duration values, or easing curves. 'Spring-in' is not a CSS-native concept — CSS doesn't have spring physics. The spec must clarify whether this is a cubic-bezier approximation of spring (and provide the exact values) or requires the Web Animations API with a spring solver.
objections.suggested_fix: Define each animation concretely. Example: spring-in: cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) 300ms on transform:scale(0.9)->scale(1) + opacity:0->1. sheet-lift: cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1) 350ms on transform:translateY(100%)->translateY(0). breathe: @keyframes with scale(1)->scale(1.02)->scale(1) over 3s infinite. All durations in CSS custom properties (--motion-duration-fast: 150ms, --motion-duration-normal: 300ms, etc.).

objections.description: File layout mixes concerns: icons.js as a flat module will grow unbounded. Every SVG icon as a JS export in one file becomes a monolith that defeats any code-splitting benefit mentioned in section 11.
objections.suggested_fix: Split icons into src/frontend/web/app/icons/{home,timeline,search,actions,chevron,status-dot,...}.js — one default export per icon. Or if using esbuild (per objection 1), tree-shaking handles this automatically from a barrel file.

suggestions.description: Home screen says 'skeleton placeholders' but doesn't specify skeleton shape fidelity. High-fidelity skeletons (matching actual content dimensions) dramatically reduce perceived layout shift vs. generic gray bars. Specify that skeleton components mirror the exact height/width/border-radius of their resolved content counterparts.

Accessibility section doesn't mention error announcement strategy. Form validation errors in the action sheet must be announced via aria-live='assertive' or role='alert'. Status updates (section 10 mentions aria-live) should use aria-live='polite' for non-blocking updates. Be explicit about which regions use which politeness level.

No mention of error states for API failures. Every screen needs a defined error state: what does Home look like when fetchMetrics() 500s? What does Timeline show when the endpoint is unreachable? Skeleton-only loading is great, but 'skeleton forever' is worse than a spinner. Define timeout-to-error-state thresholds (e.g., 8s timeout → error state with retry action).

No state management approach specified. state.js is listed but its pattern is undefined. For this app scale, I'd recommend React context + useReducer for global state (theme, auth, active tab) and colocated useState for component-local state. Explicitly ban prop drilling past 2 levels. If data fetching needs caching/deduplication, specify whether you're rolling a minimal fetch cache or using something like SWR (adds a dependency but solves stale-while-revalidate correctly).

objections.description: Conflicting Information Architecture: Timeline is listed as both a persistent bottom tab (top-level destination) and a Detail view screen (context-specific drill-down). This violates basic IA principles—tabs represent parallel destinations, not pages in a flow. If Timeline is a detail view for a selected document, it cannot also be a main tab without creating navigation paradoxes (e.g., which document loads when tapping the Timeline tab from Home?).
objections.suggested_fix: Remove Timeline from the tab bar. Reserve tabs for true peers: Home, Search, Library/History, Settings. Timeline remains a drill-down view accessed from Home or Search results. Replace the fourth tab with 'Activity' or 'Library' if a chronological collection view is needed as a primary destination.

objections.description: Missing Navigation Paradigm for Drill-Down Views: The spec defines a tab bar for primary navigation but omits how users navigate back from Detail/Timeline or Action Sheet screens. Without explicit back affordances, stack management, or URL-based routing strategy, users relying on browser back may lose application state or encounter unexpected behaviors in this SPA architecture.
objections.suggested_fix: Define a stack-based navigation pattern with explicit back buttons/chevrons in top-left of Detail and Action screens. Implement History API routing with fallback (or hash-based) to support deep linking: / (Home), /doc/:id (Detail), /action/:type (Action Sheet). Ensure focus management announces route changes to screen readers.

objections.location: Navigation section (point 3) and Action sheet section (point 6)
objections.description: Bottom Congestion Ergonomics: Combining a persistent 4-tab bottom bar with bottom sheets for secondary actions creates 'thumb competition'—two layers of controls occupying the critical lower 200px of the viewport. This forces users to navigate around the tab bar to interact with sheet content, violating mobile ergonomic principles (thumb zone heatmaps show 25% of interactions occur in the bottom 72px).
objections.suggested_fix: Eliminate the 'Actions' tab if actions are primarily initiated via bottom sheets. Reserve the tab bar for 3 items maximum (Home, Search, Library). Move secondary actions to contextual inline buttons or top-right affordances. Bottom sheets should only be used for primary workflows (e.g., 'New Search'), not secondary options.

objections.description: Playful Motion Vocabulary Conflicts with Brand Direction: The proposed animations ('breathe', 'sway', 'float') imply organic, decorative, playful characteristics. The convergence criteria explicitly mandate defaulting to 'premium/confident Nubank + Flighty over playful choices.' These motion descriptors suggest unnecessary chrome that distracts from data density and confidence.
objections.suggested_fix: Replace decorative motion names with functional, precise terminology: 'snap' (enter), 'glide' (transition), 'reveal' (expand). Use cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) for expo-out curves (Nubank-style) instead of ease/linear. Remove 'breathe' (infinite animation) as it creates visual noise; use static states with micro-interactions on interaction only.

objections.description: External CDN Dependency for Core Framework: Loading React 18 from CDN introduces a single point of failure for a production system served by web_server.py. If the CDN is unreachable or compromised, the application fails to hydrate, leaving users with a blank shell (no progressive enhancement fallback mentioned). This contradicts the 'production' requirement.
objections.suggested_fix: Self-host React and ReactDOM ES modules in src/frontend/web/vendor/ during the migration. Update import maps to reference local paths. This ensures the frontend remains functional as long as the backend (web_server.py) is operational, matching the reliability expectations of the existing Alpine implementation.

objections.description: Undiscoverable Interaction Patterns: Long-press quick actions and swipe affordances lack visual indicators. On mobile web, long-press triggers browser context menus (iOS Safari, Chrome Android), creating platform conflicts. Swipe without visible handles violates Nielsen's heuristic of 'recognition over recall.'
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit 'more' buttons (•••) for quick actions instead of long-press. Use visible drag handles (≡) on timeline items to indicate swipeability. Alternatively, remove swipe gestures entirely in favor of tap-to-expand patterns which have higher discoverability.

suggestions.description: Add requirement for 'skip to content' links and route announcement regions. Since this is an SPA without page reloads, implement a live region that announces view transitions (e.g., 'Navigated to Document Detail') for screen reader users.

Define the 'Primary KPI rail' explicitly: limit to 3 metrics maximum to prevent cognitive overload. Specify that the 'live status card' updates via aria-live='polite' to prevent interrupting screen reader users with every data refresh.

objections.description: Typography stack is undefined. 'Premium/confident' aesthetic cannot be achieved with generic system-ui fonts alone due to rendering inconsistencies across Windows/macOS/Android. Flighty/Nubank styles rely on specific geometric sans-serif characteristics.
objections.suggested_fix: Explicitly define font-family stack in tokens.css (e.g., 'Inter', 'SF Pro Display', system-ui) and include font-loading strategy (e.g., Google Fonts or self-hosted woff2) in the file layout.

objections.description: Color semantics are too abstract ('accent', 'status'). Nubank/Flighty aesthetic requires specific brand hues and surface elevation colors to create depth without heavy shadows. Vague tokens will lead to generic Material-like appearance.
objections.suggested_fix: Define specific HSL/Hex values for brand primary, surface-1/2/3, and border colors in the spec. Example: --color-brand: #820ad1 (Nubank purple) or high-contrast monochrome.

objections.description: Using React.createElement manually without JSX or a lightweight alternative (like htm) increases verbosity, raising the risk of class-name drift and visual inconsistency across components.
objections.suggested_fix: Include 'htm' or similar tagged template literal library in the import map to allow JSX-like syntax without a build step, ensuring cleaner component structure for visual consistency.

objections.description: SVG icons are mentioned but no icon set is specified. Inconsistent stroke widths and optical sizing will break visual hierarchy and accessibility.
objections.suggested_fix: Lock a specific icon library (e.g., Phosphor Icons or Heroicons) with a defined stroke width (e.g., 1.5px) and size scale in the spec.

suggestions.description: Define specific cubic-bezier values for 'confident' motion (e.g., [0.2, 0, 0, 1]) in tokens to avoid default ease-in-out which feels generic/playful.
Add a utility module 'utils.js' with a class-merging function (like clsx) to handle conditional styling cleanly without bloating component logic.
Explicitly forbid inline styles for dynamic values; enforce CSS variable usage for all dynamic visual changes to maintain theme switching integrity.

objections.description: CDN-based React ES modules without a bundler violates production performance requirements. This creates: (1) multiple sequential network requests for each module dependency chain, (2) no tree-shaking or dead-code elimination, (3) no minification, (4) no code-splitting that actually reduces initial payload. For a 'premium' experience, we need either a proper build step (Vite/esbuild) or a significantly more detailed strategy for how module loading will be optimized.
objections.suggested_fix: Either add Vite/esbuild as a zero-config build step with output to src/frontend/web/dist/, OR provide explicit HTTP/2 push headers strategy, preload hints, and module graph analysis showing total request count under 10 for initial paint.

objections.description: Spec prohibits 'ease/linear timing' but provides no actual timing curves. 'Spring-in, float, breathe, sway' are descriptive names without cubic-bezier values. This is insufficient for implementation—developers will default to guessing or use prohibited timing functions.
objections.suggested_fix: Define concrete timing functions: e.g., spring-out: cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1), smooth-out: cubic-bezier(0.33, 1, 0.68, 1), etc. Provide at least 4 curves with their exact cubic-bezier values and recommended durations.

objections.description: Spec asks reviewers to approve 'before coding' but the artifact IS the spec and it lacks the concrete component map, token map, and data flow details it claims to deliver. This is circular—approving would mean approving a promise to deliver specifications later.
objections.suggested_fix: Provide the actual component map showing hierarchy, props interfaces, and composition patterns; the actual token map with naming conventions and values; and the data flow diagram or state shape specification.

objections.description: 'Progressive cascade' loading mentioned without specifying loading priorities, request parallelization, or skeleton-to-content transition behavior. No skeleton component specifications (size, shape, animation).
objections.suggested_fix: Define: (1) explicit loading phases with priorities, (2) skeleton component variants (text, card, image), (3) content reveal animation (fade-in? stagger?), (4) maximum skeleton display duration before error state.

objections.description: 'Dark-first CSS variables' with 'light theme as override' is correct but no flash-of-wrong-theme prevention strategy. First paint may flash light theme before JS executes to detect preference. No persistence strategy (localStorage vs cookie) for theme choice.
objections.suggested_fix: Add critical-path theme detection script in <head> before React loads. Specify localStorage key naming and fallback behavior for private browsing modes.

objections.description: Breakpoints 375/768/1440 mentioned but no implementation strategy. Are these Tailwind responsive prefixes? CSS custom properties? Container queries? Mixed approach? No fluid typography scale between breakpoints.
objections.suggested_fix: Specify: (1) breakpoint token names with exact values, (2) container query vs media query decision matrix, (3) fluid sizing formula (clamp calculations) for typography and spacing between breakpoints.

suggestions.description: Add error-boundary.js component and loading-error-state.js component to handle React render errors and API failures gracefully at the shell level.
Add specific debounce timing for autocomplete (150ms recommended) and request cancellation AbortController pattern in api.js specification.
Add components/focus-trap.js and components/portal.js as utilities required for accessible bottom-sheet implementation per WCAG 2.1 focus management requirements.

-Production Frontend Implementation Spec — v2 (Revised for Convergence)
+Production Frontend Implementation Spec — v3 (Convergence Round 3)
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+0. PRE-MIGRATION AUDIT & BACKEND ENDPOINT MAPPING
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+Before any frontend code is written, execute this checklist:
+
+Step 1: Audit web_server.py routes.
+  - List every route handler in web_server.py.
+  - Map each existing endpoint to the api.js fetch function it will serve.
+  - If backend uses /api/v1/search, api.js calls /api/v1/search — the frontend adapts to the backend, never the reverse.
+  - Document the exact mapping in a ROUTES.md file at repo root.
+
+Step 2: Confirm static-file serving behavior.
+  - Verify web_server.py serves index.html for GET / with no path-based routing for sub-pages.
+  - Confirm it serves static assets from src/frontend/web/ (CSS, JS, fonts, images).
+  - If web_server.py has catch-all or path-based HTML serving, document it — this affects routing strategy.
+
+Step 3: Verify API health endpoint exists.
+  - If GET /api/health (or equivalent) exists, map it. If not, flag for backend team to add.
+  - api.js uses this on Shell mount to distinguish "backend unreachable" from "specific endpoint failed."
+
+Step 4: Runtime 404 detection.
+  - api.js must log a clear console.error with the endpoint path when it receives a 404.
+  - The error message must say: "Endpoint not found: GET /api/foo — verify backend route mapping in ROUTES.md"
+  - Do NOT silently collapse 404s into the generic error state.
+
+Migration sequence (big-bang replacement — no feature flags):
+  Step M1: Copy current index.html → _legacy/index-alpine.html (preservation, not coexistence).
+  Step M2: Create new index.html with React mount point, theme script, CSS links.
+  Step M3: Build React app → dist/. web_server.py serves new index.html at / unchanged.
+  Step M4: Smoke-test all API routes from new frontend against running backend.
+  Step M5: Remove _legacy/ after 2 weeks of production stability.
+  Rollback: revert index.html to _legacy/index-alpine.html. Zero backend changes required.
 
 ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 1. BUILD SYSTEM & MODULE STRATEGY
 ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 
 Build tool: esbuild (single dependency, zero-config).
-Command: esbuild src/frontend/web/src/main.jsx --bundle --splitting --format=esm --outdir=src/frontend/web/dist --minify --target=es2020
+
+Decision: esbuild bundles React into the output. No vendor/ directory. No import maps.
+Rationale: import maps require Safari 16.4+ / Firefox 108+ minimum. Bundling React eliminates
+the compatibility risk entirely and simplifies the build to a single command. The bundle size
+difference is zero since React ends up in the output either way.
+
+Command:
+  esbuild src/frontend/web/src/main.jsx \
+    --bundle \
+    --format=esm \
+    --outdir=src/frontend/web/dist \
+    --minify \
+    --target=es2020 \
+    --metafile=meta.json
+
+No --splitting in v1 (splitting requires import maps for shared chunks in browsers;
+we just eliminated import maps). Single entry bundle. Code-split in v2 via dynamic import()
+if bundle exceeds budget.
+
+Bundle budget enforcement:
+  CI step parses meta.json, fails build if dist/main.js gzipped > 80KB.
+  Budget breakdown (estimated):
+    React 18 + ReactDOM:  ~42KB gzipped
+    App components:       ~15KB gzipped
+    Icons (tree-shaken):  ~3KB gzipped
+    CSS (tokens + anim):  ~4KB gzipped (loaded separately, not in JS budget)
+    Total JS:             ~60KB gzipped (20KB headroom to 80KB cap)
+
+Preact escape hatch: if bundle exceeds 80KB, switch via esbuild alias:
+  --alias:react=preact/compat --alias:react-dom=preact/compat
+  Preact compat is ~4KB vs React's 42KB. Document this as v1.1 option, not default.
+
 Source code lives in src/frontend/web/src/ (JSX, full React idioms).
-Built output lands in src/frontend/web/dist/ (static ES modules, minified, tree-shaken, code-split).
+Built output lands in src/frontend/web/dist/ (static ES module, minified, tree-shaken).
 index.html references dist/main.js via <script type="module">.
-No dev server. No HMR. No webpack. One deterministic build command.
-React 18 and ReactDOM self-hosted in src/frontend/web/vendor/ (no CDN dependency).
-Import map in index.html resolves "react" and "react-dom" to local vendor paths.
 web_server.py serves src/frontend/web/ unchanged — only static file additions.
 
+Browser support floor: Chrome 89+, Safari 15+, Firefox 90+, Edge 89+.
+

+  class ApiError extends Error {
+    constructor(status, message, retryable, endpoint) { ... }
+  }
+  - All fetchers throw ApiError({ status, message, retryable, endpoint }) on failure.
+  - 8-second timeout via AbortController.timeout(8000) — on timeout, throw with retryable: true.
+  - On 404: console.error(`Endpoint not found: ${method} ${url} — verify backend route mapping in ROUTES.md`)
+  - AbortError handling: if error.name === 'AbortError', the caller returns silently without updating state.
+    This is critical for SearchComposer — cancelled requests must not trigger error states.
+  - Callers catch and render <ErrorState message onRetry /> (retryable) or <ErrorState message /> (non-retryable).

+Health check on Shell mount:
+  - Shell calls fetchHealthCheck() before firing screen-specific fetches.
+  - If health check fails: full-screen <ErrorState message="Cannot reach server" onRetry /> — no individual skeletons.
+  - If health check succeeds: proceed to screen-specific data loading.
+
+Autocomplete debounce: 200ms in SearchComposer. Previous in-flight request cancelled via AbortController before new request fires. AbortError is silently swallowed (see above).

-11. SCREEN SPECIFICATIONS
-═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-
-─── HOME SCREEN (#/) ───
-
-Loading phases:
-  Phase 1 (immediate, 0ms): Shell renders with header ("Dashboard"), KPI rail skeleton (3 cards: 80×48px rounded-md), status card skeleton (full-width 96px rounded-lg).
-  Phase 2 (parallel fetch): fetchMetrics() + fetchStatus() fire simultaneously.
-  Phase 3 (on resolve): skeleton → content with .motion-glide + stagger. KPI rail max 3 metrics.
-  Phase 4 (deferred, after Phase 3 paint): fetchPresets(), fetchHistory() for lower sections.
-  Error: if Phase 2 fails after 8s timeout → replace skeleton with <ErrorState message="Unable to load dashboard" onRetry />.
-
-Skeleton fidelity: skeleton components match exact dimensions of resolved content (KPI card skeleton = same height/width/border-radius as KPIMetric). No generic gray bars.
-
-Content reveal: .motion-glide with .motion-stagger-{1,2,3} on sequential cards.
-
-StatusCard: live-updates via polling (30s interval) with aria-live="polite" region. State renders colored dot (--status-*) + text.
-
-─── SEARCH SCREEN (#/search) ───
-
-SearchComposer: sticky top, input with 200ms debounce, AbortController cancels previous in-flight. Preset chips below input.
-Results: list of Result cards, each with title, snippet, timestamp, status badge. Tap navigates to #/doc/:id.
-Loading: skeleton list (5 items, each 72px height matching Result card).
-Empty: <EmptyState message="No results found" />.
-Error: <ErrorState onRetry />.
-
-─── LIBRARY SCREEN (#/library) ───
-
-Chronological list of past searches/interactions.
-Loading: skeleton list.
-Empty: <EmptyState message="No history yet" />.
-
-─── DETAIL SCREEN (#/doc/:id) ───
-
-Header: sticky, shows document title + status badge. Back chevron top-left.
-Timeline: vertical line with TimelineItem nodes. Each node: colored dot (state-based), timestamp, label, optional detail expansion.
-Quick actions: "⋯" button in header triggers <BottomSheet> with action list.
-Loading: skeleton with sticky header placeholder + 5 timeline item skeletons.
-Error: <ErrorState onRetry />.
