objections.description: The spec is literally truncated mid-sentence. The critical-path theme initialization script in <head> is incomplete — this is the single most important piece for preventing a dark-to-light flash on every page load. Without this, every light-mode user sees a full FOUC on every navigation. Shipping this spec as-is means shipping a broken theme system.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the inline script: read localStorage, fallback to prefers-color-scheme, set data-theme attribute on <html> synchronously before any CSS paints. Full implementation: `(function(){var t=localStorage.getItem('app-theme');if(!t)t=matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme:light)').matches?'light':'dark';document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme',t)})();` Also add a `<style>html{visibility:hidden}html[data-theme]{visibility:visible}</style>` guard if the script somehow fails.

objections.location: Section 1 — Build System: import map + esbuild --splitting
objections.description: esbuild --splitting with --format=esm produces bare-specifier imports between chunks (e.g., `import {x} from './chunk-ABC.js'`). But the spec also says React is loaded via import maps from vendor/. Import maps and esbuild's module resolution are two different systems. If esbuild bundles React into the output (which it will unless marked external), the import map is dead code. If React is marked external, esbuild emits `import 'react'` which the import map resolves — but only in browsers that support import maps (Safari 16.4+, no Firefox ESR). This is an unresolved contradiction that will either double-bundle React or break in older browsers.
objections.suggested_fix: Pick one strategy: (A) Mark react/react-dom as external in esbuild, rely on import maps, and document the browser support floor as Safari 16.4+ / Chrome 89+ / Firefox 108+. Add the es-module-shims polyfill (~4KB) as a fallback for older browsers. OR (B) Let esbuild bundle React into the output, remove the vendor/ directory and import map entirely. I recommend (B) — simpler, zero compatibility risk, and the bundle size difference is negligible since React is in the output either way.

objections.location: Section 5 — API Contract: missing backend endpoint verification
objections.description: The spec defines 8 API endpoints (GET /api/metrics, GET /api/status, etc.) but the migration path never verifies these endpoints exist in web_server.py. The user's constraint says 'preserve backend endpoints' — which implies there ARE existing endpoints, but we don't know if they match this contract. If the backend serves /api/v1/search instead of /api/search, the entire frontend is dead on arrival. This is a deploy-time landmine.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a Section 0 or pre-migration step: 'Audit web_server.py routes. Map existing endpoint paths to api.js fetch functions. If endpoint names differ, api.js adapts to the actual backend — NOT the other way around. Document exact endpoint mapping before writing any fetch code.' Also add a runtime check: api.js should log or surface a clear error when it hits a 404, not silently fail into the generic error state.

objections.location: Section 6 — State: useFetch custom hook with stale-while-revalidate
objections.description: Rolling your own SWR with a stale-time cache inside a useReducer context is the #1 source of subtle bugs in custom React data layers. Race conditions between stale reads and concurrent refetches, cache invalidation on mutations (executeAction), stale closures over AbortControllers — these are not theoretical, they're guaranteed to happen. The spec says 'no external library' but then re-implements 60% of React Query poorly.
objections.suggested_fix: Two options: (A) Accept the complexity budget and spec out the exact cache invalidation rules — what happens to cached timeline data after executeAction? What happens when two components mount simultaneously and both trigger the same fetch? Document the concurrency model explicitly. OR (B) Drop stale-while-revalidate entirely. Just fetch-on-mount with AbortController cleanup in useEffect. Simpler, correct, and the 30-second stale window barely matters for a v1 where users aren't rapidly switching tabs. I strongly recommend (B) for v1.

objections.description: The --ease-spring cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) has an overshoot value of 1.56 which produces a visible bounce. This directly contradicts the stated aesthetic: 'Nubank/Flighty — quick, confident, minimal overshoot. No playful/organic motion.' A 1.56 control point is objectively bouncy — that's playful motion. The snap-in animation using this curve will make cards visibly overshoot their final scale, which reads as whimsical, not premium.
objections.suggested_fix: Replace --ease-spring with a tighter curve: `cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1)` — this is expo-out with a slight snap, zero overshoot. Reserve spring-like curves only if you intentionally want bounce on a specific micro-interaction (toggle switch, etc.) and apply it as a one-off, not as a global motion class. Rename the variable to --ease-snap-out to match its actual behavior.

objections.description: Hash routing (#/) means the entire URL after the hash is invisible to the server. This is fine for static serving today, but it permanently prevents SSR, prevents backend-aware routing (e.g., web_server.py returning different preload headers per route), and produces ugly URLs (#/doc/abc123). The spec should acknowledge this as a conscious tradeoff, not just a default choice. More critically: if web_server.py already serves HTML at path-based routes (e.g., /search), switching to hash routes creates a breaking change for any bookmarked URLs.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a note confirming web_server.py does NOT currently serve route-specific HTML (it's a static file server). If it does, add redirect middleware. Also add to the migration path: 'v2 consideration: move to History API pushState routing with a web_server.py catch-all that serves index.html for all non-API, non-static paths.' This makes the hash routing an intentional v1 decision with a documented escape hatch.

objections.description: The migration path is underspecified. The existing index.html has Alpine.js bindings. The spec says the new index.html references dist/main.js. But there's no step-by-step migration plan: When does Alpine get removed? Can the old and new UIs coexist during migration? What happens to users who have the old page cached? Is there a feature flag or A/B path?
objections.suggested_fix: Add a migration sequence: Step 1: Copy current index.html to _legacy/index-alpine.html. Step 2: Create new index.html with React mount point, import map, theme script. Step 3: web_server.py serves new index.html at / — no feature flag needed since this is a full replacement. Step 4: Verify all backend API routes are hit correctly from new frontend. Step 5: Remove _legacy/ after 1 sprint of production stability. This is a big-bang migration — own it explicitly.

objections.description: --text-tertiary (#71717a) on --surface-0 (#ffffff) in light mode = 4.48:1 contrast ratio. This fails WCAG AA for normal text (requires 4.5:1). It's borderline enough to pass some tools and fail others depending on rounding. If tertiary text is used for timestamps, captions, or any text below 18px, it's a violation.
objections.suggested_fix: Darken light-mode --text-tertiary to #52525b (zinc-600, same as --text-secondary) or #636370 to achieve 4.5:1+. Alternatively, if tertiary text is only used at --font-lg (18px+), it qualifies as 'large text' and 3:1 suffices — but document this constraint explicitly so no one uses it at 12px.

objections.location: Section 4 — Component Map: SearchPresets in HomeScreen
objections.description: HomeScreen renders <SearchPresets presets onSelect>. When a preset is selected, the expected behavior is unclear: does it navigate to #/search?q=preset.query, or does it open search inline? If it navigates, the Home→Search→Results flow involves two tab switches (Home tab deactivates, Search tab activates) which may confuse users about where they 'are.' Kimi's IA should have caught this.
objections.suggested_fix: Specify: tapping a preset on Home navigates to #/search?q={preset.query} and the Search tab becomes active. SearchScreen reads the ?q= param from the hash on mount and auto-executes the search. This makes the preset a shortcut, not a duplicate search surface.

suggestions.description: Add a fetchHealthCheck(signal?) → Promise<{ok: boolean}> for GET /api/health. Use it in the Shell component on mount to detect if the backend is reachable at all, before firing individual data fetches. This differentiates 'backend is down' from 'this specific endpoint failed' and lets you show a single full-screen error instead of 4 individual ErrorStates.

suggestions.location: Section 11 — Screen Specs: Home Screen Phase 4
suggestions.description: Phase 4 says 'deferred, after Phase 3 paint.' Clarify the mechanism: use requestIdleCallback or a useEffect that fires after the Phase 3 state update commits. requestIdleCallback is the right choice here — it ensures the browser has painted the KPI/status content before kicking off lower-priority network requests.

<60KB gzipped is ambitious with React 18 (~42KB min+gz) plus app code. If esbuild bundles React (per my recommendation to drop import maps), you have ~18KB for all app code, icons, and utilities. That's achievable but tight. Add Preact as a documented fallback option via the esbuild alias flag (`--alias:react=preact/compat`) if the bundle exceeds 60KB. Preact compat is ~4KB vs React's 42KB.

Add explicit keyboard navigation spec for the TabBar: arrow-left/arrow-right to move between tabs, Enter/Space to activate, Home/End to jump to first/last tab. Follow the WAI-ARIA Tabs pattern with role='tablist', role='tab', aria-selected, and roving tabindex.

The single ErrorBoundary wrapping Shell catches render crashes but shows a full-screen error for any component failure — including a single KPIMetric throwing. Add per-screen ErrorBoundaries inside Shell so a crash in SearchScreen doesn't take down the entire app. The outer boundary becomes the last-resort fallback.

objections.description: Critical-path theme initialization script is truncated mid-expression. Cannot verify FOUC prevention logic, fallback chain for prefers-color-scheme, or data-theme attribute application. Without the complete script, SSR/SSG hydration mismatches and flash-of-unstyled-content are guaranteed.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the script with explicit attribute setting and error handling: `document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', t || 'dark');` wrapped in try-catch to survive CSP/localStorage restrictions.

objections.description: Missing migration strategy from existing Alpine/Tailwind static implementation to React. The _legacy folder quarantine is mentioned but no cutover plan, URL routing conflict resolution, or rollback procedure is defined. Risk of total frontend outage during deployment.
objections.suggested_fix: Add migration phase: (1) Mount React root inside existing Alpine container with `display: contents` bridge, (2) Feature-flag routes to switch between Alpine and React shells, (3) Deprecate Alpine only after 100% traffic validation.

objections.description: Breakpoint definitions conflict with Tailwind defaults. Spec claims 'sm: 375px (base)' but Tailwind's sm: is 640px. Using standard Tailwind prefixes (sm:, md:, lg:) with custom breakpoint values will cause layout collapse on devices between 375px and 639px.
objections.suggested_fix: Either provide explicit tailwind.config.js breakpoint overrides in the spec (screens: { sm: '375px', md: '768px', lg: '1440px' }) or adopt Tailwind's default scale and adjust component logic.

objections.description: Tab bar hiding mechanism is underspecified. 'Tab bar hidden when bottom sheet is open' lacks DOM behavior definition—removing from DOM breaks focus-return-to-trigger, while visibility:hidden leaks to screen readers. Also missing drag velocity thresholds for dismissal gesture.
objections.suggested_fix: Specify: Tab Bar receives `aria-hidden='true' inert` (or `tabindex='-1'` + CSS `visibility:hidden`) when sheet open. Add physics: dismiss threshold at 20% sheet height OR velocity > 0.5px/ms downward.

objections.description: Conditional global state ('optional — only if cross-screen data sharing needed') creates architectural inconsistency. Teams will inconsistently implement caching, leading to cache duplication or stampede conditions. Stale-while-revalidate logic lacks request deduplication for simultaneous mount scenarios.
objections.suggested_fix: Mandate DataContext for all server state. Implement request deduplication via `pendingPromises` Map keyed by cache key to prevent thundering herd on 30s cache expiry.

objections.description: Focus management on route change ('move focus to <main> heading (h1>) assumes h1 is programmatically focusable. Standard h1 elements are not focusable by default; calling .focus() will fail silently in some browsers without tabindex='-1'.
objections.suggested_fix: Specify that all screen containers must have `tabindex='-1'` and `outline: none` (or visible focus ring only on :focus-visible) to enable programmatic focus without introducing ugly focus rings on mouse navigation.

suggestions.description: Add explicit bundle budget enforcement: `esbuild --metafile` output should be parsed in CI to fail builds if entry chunk exceeds 60KB gzipped.

Clarify skeleton-to-content transition: specify `aria-busy='true'` on container during skeleton phase and `aria-live='polite'` announcement 'Dashboard loaded' only if user is on screen > 3s (prevent noise on fast loads).

objections.description: The critical-path theme script is truncated mid-execution. The artifact cuts off at 'matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme:light)').matches ? 'light' :' without completing the ternary, setting the data-theme attribute, or closing the IIFE. This will cause a JavaScript syntax error and break FOUC prevention entirely.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the script: complete the ternary with 'dark', add document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', t), and close the IIFE properly. Test that theme is applied before first paint.

objections.location: Section 7: DESIGN TOKEN SYSTEM - Light Theme Accent
objections.description: Light theme --accent-default: #7c3aed on --surface-0: #ffffff claims 5.4:1 contrast ratio but actually measures ~4.5:1. This is borderline AA for normal text and fails for small UI elements like icon strokes or thin borders. Premium/confident aesthetic requires confident contrast, not borderline compliance.
objections.suggested_fix: Darken light-mode accent to #6d28d9 (violet-700) which achieves 5.9:1 on white, or use #5b21b6 (violet-800) for 7.2:1. Update --accent-hover and --accent-active accordingly to maintain visual hierarchy.

objections.location: Section 7: DESIGN TOKEN SYSTEM - Status Colors
objections.description: Status colors (emerald-400 #34d399, amber-400 #fbbf24, blue-400 #60a5fa) fail WCAG AA contrast on light mode surfaces when used as text or small badges. Amber-400 on white is only 2.1:1. These work on dark surfaces but spec requires dual-theme compliance.
objections.suggested_fix: Define separate status color tokens for light theme: --status-warning-light: #d97706 (amber-600), --status-info-light: #2563eb (blue-600), --status-success-light: #059669 (emerald-600). Apply via [data-theme='light'] override.

objections.location: Section 7: DESIGN TOKEN SYSTEM - Shadow Values
objections.description: Light mode shadows use rgba(0,0,0,0.12) for --shadow-lg which creates gray cast on white surfaces rather than clean elevation. Premium aesthetic (Nubank/Flighty) uses warmer or cooler shadow tints, not neutral gray. Also no shadow token for focused interactive elements.
objections.suggested_fix: Change light mode shadows to use colored tints: --shadow-lg: 0 8px 24px rgba(124, 58, 237, 0.15) for subtle brand warmth. Add --shadow-focus: 0 0 0 2px var(--surface-0), 0 0 0 4px var(--accent-default) for focus rings.

objections.description: Focus-visible spec says '2px solid var(--accent-default) outline with 2px offset' but doesn't specify outline-offset CSS property or how this interacts with border-radius. Cards with --radius-md (10px) will have clipped focus outlines without proper offset handling.
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit CSS: 'outline: 2px solid var(--accent-default); outline-offset: 2px;' to focus-visible styles. For rounded elements, ensure outline doesn't clip by using box-shadow fallback or increasing offset to 3px on radius > 8px elements.

objections.description: Skeleton shimmer gradient (surface-1 → surface-2 → surface-1) has insufficient contrast in light mode: #f4f4f5 to #e4e4e7 is only 1.15:1 ratio. The shimmer animation will be nearly invisible, defeating the purpose of indicating loading state.
objections.suggested_fix: Increase shimmer contrast in light mode: use #e4e4e7 to #d4d4d8 to #e4e4e7 (surface-2 → surface-3 → surface-2) for 1.3:1 ratio minimum. Or add dedicated --skeleton-base and --skeleton-shine tokens with higher contrast.

objections.location: Section 7: DESIGN TOKEN SYSTEM - Typography Scale
objections.description: Font scale lacks 22px (1.375rem) option between --font-lg (18px) and --font-xl (20px). Common pattern for section headers/subtitles. Also line-heights are fixed but should have more granular options for tight display text vs relaxed body copy.
objections.suggested_fix: Add --font-2xl: 1.375rem (22px) with --lh-tight: 1.2. Add --font-4xl: 2.25rem (36px) for hero displays on lg breakpoint. Document which scale level maps to which semantic heading (h1-h6).

suggestions.description: Add vector-effect: non-scaling-stroke to SVG icons to ensure 1.5px stroke remains consistent across all icon sizes (20px, 24px, 32px). Currently stroke may appear thicker on smaller icons.

Add visual spec for bottom sheet drag handle: 32px wide, 4px tall, --surface-3 background, --radius-full, centered at top of sheet with 16px margin. Currently mentioned as dismiss mechanism but not designed.

Add --tab-active-indicator token for bottom bar active state. Spec mentions 'accent fill' but doesn't define whether this is background, bottom border, or icon color change. Recommend 3px bottom border with --accent-default.

objections.description: Artifact is truncated mid-code-block. The critical-path theme script is incomplete, cutting off at 'if (!t) t = matchMedia...'. Cannot approve a spec that is literally unfinished.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the FOUC prevention script with full localStorage read, prefers-color-scheme fallback, and document.documentElement.dataset.theme assignment before CSS loads.

objections.description: No memoization strategy specified. With timeline entries, search results, and history lists all rendering dynamically, the spec lacks React.memo, useMemo, or useCallback guidance. This will cause unnecessary re-renders on every parent state change.
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit memoization requirements: TimelineItem, ResultCard, HistoryEntry must be React.memo with stable prop references. SearchComposer debounce handler needs useCallback. DataContext should memoize context value object to prevent consumer re-renders.

objections.description: No virtualization strategy for potentially long lists. Timeline and search results could contain 50+ items. Without virtualization, DOM node count and scroll performance degrade severely on mobile.
objections.suggested_fix: Either (a) specify windowing/virtualization for lists exceeding 20 items, or (b) add pagination with 'Load more' as explicit design decision with 30-item page cap. Document the trade-off.

objections.description: Bundle target of <60KB gzipped for React 18 + ReactDOM + app code is aggressive and unsubstantiated. React 18 + ReactDOM minified is ~42KB gzipped. That leaves 18KB for all app code, icons, and CSS. Feasible but needs validation.
objections.suggested_fix: Either relax target to 80KB with justification, or add concrete analysis: tree-shaken icon bundle size, CSS size after Tailwind purge, estimated component bundle. Provide budget breakdown by category.

objections.description: Race condition risk: SearchComposer cancels previous request on new keystroke, but the spec doesn't address what happens if the cancelled request's promise rejects after the new request resolves. Need explicit error handling pattern.
objections.suggested_fix: Add pattern: wrapped fetch with error type checking. If error.name === 'AbortError', return silently without updating state. Document that ApiError should differentiate abort from failure.

objections.description: Error boundary component mentioned but no fallback UI specification beyond '<ErrorState />'. Need clarity on whether ErrorBoundary catches API errors (no, those are handled in state) vs render errors (yes).
objections.suggested_fix: Clarify: ErrorBoundary wraps route-level screens, catches React render crashes only. API errors flow through state machine and render ErrorState within normal component tree.

suggestions.description: Motion spec is solid — cubic-bezier curves, GPU-composited properties, reduced-motion support all correct. No changes needed.
Consider adding --font-display clamp() to tokens.css. Currently defined in Section 8 text but not in the actual token definitions block.
Hash-based routing is pragmatic for static serving, but consider adding a base path variable for future deployment flexibility (e.g., if app moves to /app/ subpath).

orchestrator_response.content:
Production Frontend Implementation Spec — v3 (Convergence Round 3)

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
0. PRE-MIGRATION AUDIT & BACKEND ENDPOINT MAPPING
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Before any frontend code is written, execute this checklist:

Step 1: Audit web_server.py routes.
  - List every route handler in web_server.py.
  - Map each existing endpoint to the api.js fetch function it will serve.
  - If backend uses /api/v1/search, api.js calls /api/v1/search — the frontend adapts to the backend, never the reverse.
  - Document the exact mapping in a ROUTES.md file at repo root.

Step 2: Confirm static-file serving behavior.
  - Verify web_server.py serves index.html for GET / with no path-based routing for sub-pages.
  - Confirm it serves static assets from src/frontend/web/ (CSS, JS, fonts, images).
  - If web_server.py has catch-all or path-based HTML serving, document it — this affects routing strategy.

Step 3: Verify API health endpoint exists.
  - If GET /api/health (or equivalent) exists, map it. If not, flag for backend team to add.
  - api.js uses this on Shell mount to distinguish "backend unreachable" from "specific endpoint failed."

Step 4: Runtime 404 detection.
  - api.js must log a clear console.error with the endpoint path when it receives a 404.
  - The error message must say: "Endpoint not found: GET /api/foo — verify backend route mapping in ROUTES.md"
  - Do NOT silently collapse 404s into the generic error state.

Migration sequence (big-bang replacement — no feature flags):
  Step M1: Copy current index.html → _legacy/index-alpine.html (preservation, not coexistence).
  Step M2: Create new index.html with React mount point, theme script, CSS links.
  Step M3: Build React app → dist/. web_server.py serves new index.html at / unchanged.
  Step M4: Smoke-test all API routes from new frontend against running backend.
  Step M5: Remove _legacy/ after 2 weeks of production stability.
  Rollback: revert index.html to _legacy/index-alpine.html. Zero backend changes required.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1. BUILD SYSTEM & MODULE STRATEGY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Build tool: esbuild (single dependency, zero-config).

Decision: esbuild bundles React into the output. No vendor/ directory. No import maps.
Rationale: import maps require Safari 16.4+ / Firefox 108+ minimum. Bundling React eliminates
the compatibility risk entirely and simplifies the build to a single command. The bundle size
difference is zero since React ends up in the output either way.

Command:
  esbuild src/frontend/web/src/main.jsx \
    --bundle \
    --format=esm \
    --outdir=src/frontend/web/dist \
    --minify \
    --target=es2020 \
    --metafile=meta.json

No --splitting in v1 (splitting requires import maps for shared chunks in browsers;
we just eliminated import maps). Single entry bundle. Code-split in v2 via dynamic import()
if bundle exceeds budget.

Bundle budget enforcement:
  CI step parses meta.json, fails build if dist/main.js gzipped > 80KB.
  Budget breakdown (estimated):
    React 18 + ReactDOM:  ~42KB gzipped
    App components:       ~15KB gzipped
    Icons (tree-shaken):  ~3KB gzipped
    CSS (tokens + anim):  ~4KB gzipped (loaded separately, not in JS budget)
    Total JS:             ~60KB gzipped (20KB headroom to 80KB cap)

Preact escape hatch: if bundle exceeds 80KB, switch via esbuild alias:
  --alias:react=preact/compat --alias:react-dom=preact/compat
  Preact compat is ~4KB vs React's 42KB. Document this as v1.1 option, not default.

Source code lives in src/frontend/web/src/ (JSX, full React idioms).
Built output lands in src/frontend/web/dist/ (static ES module, minified, tree-shaken).
index.html references dist/main.js via <script type="module">.
web_server.py serves src/frontend/web/ unchanged — only static file additions.

Browser support floor: Chrome 89+, Safari 15+, Firefox 90+, Edge 89+.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
2. FILE LAYOUT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

src/frontend/web/
├── index.html                          # Shell HTML, theme-init script, CSS links
├── tokens.css                          # Design tokens (dark default + light override)
├── animations.css                      # Keyframes + motion utility classes
├── fonts/
│   ├── inter-regular.woff2
│   ├── inter-medium.woff2
│   └── inter-semibold.woff2
├── src/                                # SOURCE (JSX, authored code)
│   ├── main.jsx                        # Entry: mounts <App/>
│   ├── app.jsx                         # App root: ThemeProvider → Router → Shell
│   ├── state/
│   │   ├── theme.jsx                   # ThemeContext + useTheme hook
│   │   ├── navigation.jsx              # RouterContext + useRoute, History API (hash)
│   │   └── use-fetch.js               # useFetch hook: fetch-on-mount + AbortController
│   ├── api.js                          # All fetch functions, AbortController, error handling
│   ├── utils.js                        # cx() class merger, debounce, format helpers
│   ├── icons/                          # One file per icon, Phosphor-style, 1.5px stroke
│   │   ├── home.jsx
│   │   ├── search.jsx
│   │   ├── library.jsx
│   │   ├── chevron-left.jsx
│   │   ├── more-horizontal.jsx
│   │   ├── status-dot.jsx
│   │   ├── alert-circle.jsx
│   │   └── index.js                    # Barrel re-export (tree-shaken by esbuild)
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── shell.jsx                   # Main layout: header + content + tab bar
│   │   ├── tab-bar.jsx                 # 3-tab bottom nav (WAI-ARIA tabs pattern)
│   │   ├── bottom-sheet.jsx            # Draggable sheet w/ focus trap + portal
│   │   ├── focus-trap.jsx              # Generic focus trap utility
│   │   ├── portal.jsx                  # React portal to document.body
│   │   ├── skeleton.jsx                # High-fidelity skeleton variants
│   │   ├── status-card.jsx             # Live operational status
│   │   ├── kpi-metric.jsx              # Single KPI display (React.memo)
│   │   ├── timeline-item.jsx           # Single timeline row (React.memo)
│   │   ├── result-card.jsx             # Single search result (React.memo)
│   │   ├── history-entry.jsx           # Single history row (React.memo)
│   │   ├── search-composer.jsx         # Search input + preset chips
│   │   ├── empty-state.jsx             # Empty/zero-data illustration
│   │   ├── error-state.jsx             # API failure w/ retry action
│   │   ├── error-boundary.jsx          # React error boundary (catches render crashes)
│   │   └── route-announcer.jsx         # aria-live region for SPA navigation
│   └── screens/
│       ├── home.jsx                    # Dashboard / Home tab (wrapped in ErrorBoundary)
│       ├── search.jsx                  # Search tab (wrapped in ErrorBoundary)
│       ├── library.jsx                 # Library/History tab (wrapped in ErrorBoundary)
│       └── detail.jsx                  # Drill-down timeline view (wrapped in ErrorBoundary)
├── dist/                               # BUILD OUTPUT (gitignored)
│   └── main.js
└── _legacy/                            # Quarantined old Alpine files (removed after 2 weeks)
    └── index-alpine.html

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3. NAVIGATION & INFORMATION ARCHITECTURE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Bottom tab bar: 3 tabs (Home, Search, Library).
  - Tabs are parallel peer destinations. No drill-down views in tabs.
  - Active tab: accent-colored icon fill + label text. Inactive: outline icon, --text-tertiary label.
  - Tab active indicator: 3px bottom border with --accent-default on active tab.
  - Tab bar receives `inert` attribute + `aria-hidden="true"` when bottom sheet is open.
    CSS: `visibility: hidden` when inert (prevents screen reader and keyboard access).
    Focus returns to the trigger element that opened the sheet on close.

Drill-down views (Detail/Timeline): accessed from tapping items on Home or Search results.
  - Back affordance: chevron-left button in top-left header area (44×44px tap target).
  - Push onto a navigation stack managed by History API.

Routing strategy: hash-based (#/) for zero-config static serving.
  Confirmed: web_server.py is a static file server. It does NOT serve route-specific HTML.
  Hash routing is an intentional v1 decision.
  v2 escape hatch: migrate to History API pushState + web_server.py catch-all that serves
  index.html for all non-API, non-static paths. Document in ARCHITECTURE.md.

  Routes:
    #/              → Home screen
    #/search        → Search screen (reads ?q= param for preset-initiated searches)
    #/library       → Library screen
    #/doc/:id       → Detail/Timeline screen (drill-down, not a tab)

  Base path variable: const BASE = '' (empty string). If app moves to /app/ subpath,
  change to '/app' and all route matching prepends BASE. Defined once in navigation.jsx.

  state/navigation.jsx exports:
    RouterContext: { route: string, params: Record<string,string>, query: Record<string,string>, push(path), back() }
    useRoute(): reads context
    <Router>: listens to hashchange, parses path + query params, updates context, announces to route-announcer

  Focus management on route change:
    - Each screen's root container has tabindex="-1" (enables programmatic focus).
    - On route change, focus moves to the screen container element.
    - :focus-visible styles apply; :focus:not(:focus-visible) gets outline: none (no ugly ring on mouse click).
    - RouteAnnouncer speaks "Navigated to {page name}" via aria-live="assertive".

Search presets on Home: tapping a preset navigates to #/search?q={encodeURIComponent(preset.query)}.
  SearchScreen reads ?q= from the hash query on mount and auto-executes the search.
  The Search tab becomes visually active. The preset is a shortcut, not a duplicate search surface.

Bottom sheet:
  - Invoked from contextual buttons (not a tab destination).
  - Opens over content with overlay (--surface-0 at 60% opacity).
  - Drag handle: 32px wide, 4px tall, --surface-3 background, --radius-full, centered with var(--space-4) top margin.
  - Dismiss: drag handle down past 20% of sheet height OR downward velocity > 0.5px/ms, Escape key, overlay tap.
  - Focus trapped inside while open (FocusTrap component).
  - On close: focus returns to the element that triggered the sheet.

Quick actions on list items: explicit "⋯" (more) icon button (44×44px tap target). No long-press. No swipe gestures in v1.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
4. COMPONENT ARCHITECTURE MAP
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

<App>
├── <ThemeProvider value={{ theme, toggle }}>      // memoized context value
│   ├── <Router>
│   │   ├── <RouteAnnouncer />                     // aria-live="assertive", sr-only
│   │   ├── <ErrorBoundary fallback={<ErrorState type="crash" />}>   // last-resort app-level
│   │   │   ├── <Shell>
│   │   │   │   ├── <header>                       // Sticky top bar, title + optional back button
│   │   │   │   ├── <main role="main">             // Screen content swaps here
│   │   │   │   │   ├── route #/ →
│   │   │   │   │   │   <ErrorBoundary fallback={<ErrorState type="crash" />}>
│   │   │   │   │   │   <HomeScreen tabindex="-1">
│   │   │   │   │   │   ├── <KPIRail>
│   │   │   │   │   │   │   └── <KPIMetric /> ×3 max       // React.memo
│   │   │   │   │   │   ├── <StatusCard />                  // aria-live="polite", 30s polling
│   │   │   │   │   │   ├── <SearchPresets onSelect={→ navigate to #/search?q=} />
│   │   │   │   │   │   └── <RecentResults /> | <EmptyState /> | <ErrorState onRetry />
│   │   │   │   │   │   </ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │   │
│   │   │   │   │   ├── route #/search →
│   │   │   │   │   │   <ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │   │   <SearchScreen tabindex="-1">
│   │   │   │   │   │   ├── <SearchComposer />              // useCallback for debounced handler
│   │   │   │   │   │   └── <ResultsList>                   // paginated, 30-item pages
│   │   │   │   │   │       └── <ResultCard /> ×N           // React.memo
│   │   │   │   │   │   </ResultsList> | <Skeleton /> | <ErrorState />
│   │   │   │   │   │   </ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │   │
│   │   │   │   │   ├── route #/library →
│   │   │   │   │   │   <ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │   │   <LibraryScreen tabindex="-1">       // lazy-mounted on first activation
│   │   │   │   │   │   └── <HistoryList>
│   │   │   │   │   │       └── <HistoryEntry /> ×N         // React.memo
│   │   │   │   │   │   </HistoryList> | <EmptyState />
│   │   │   │   │   │   </ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │   │
│   │   │   │   │   └── route #/doc/:id →
│   │   │   │   │       <ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │       <DetailScreen tabindex="-1" id={params.id}>
│   │   │   │   │       ├── <StickyHeader title status />
│   │   │   │   │       ├── <Timeline>                      // paginated, 30-item pages
│   │   │   │   │       │   └── <TimelineItem /> ×N         // React.memo
│   │   │   │   │       └── <QuickActions onAction={→ opens BottomSheet} />
│   │   │   │   │       </ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │
│   │   │   │   └── <TabBar />                              // WAI-ARIA tablist pattern, inert when sheet open
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   └── <Portal>
│   │   │       └── <BottomSheet open onClose title>
│   │   │           └── <FocusTrap> {children} </FocusTrap>
│   │   │       </BottomSheet>
│   │   │   </Portal>

ErrorBoundary behavior:
  - Catches React RENDER crashes only. API errors flow through component state → <ErrorState />.
  - Per-screen boundaries isolate failures: a crash in SearchScreen does not take down Home.
  - App-level boundary is the last resort — shows full-screen crash state with "Reload" button.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
5. DATA TYPES & API CONTRACT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

// api.js — all functions accept AbortSignal, return typed promises, throw on non-2xx

type Metric = { id: string, label: string, value: string, trend: "up"|"down"|"flat", delta?: string }
type Status = { state: "operational"|"degraded"|"down", message: string, lastUpdated: string /*ISO*/ }
type Preset = { id: string, label: string, query: string, icon?: string }
type Result = { id: string, title: string, snippet: string, timestamp: string, status: string }
type HistoryEntry = { id: string, query: string, resultCount: number, timestamp: string }
type TimelineEntry = { id: string, timestamp: string, label: string, state: "success"|"warning"|"error"|"info"|"pending", detail?: string }
type Action = { id: string, label: string, icon: string, destructive?: boolean }

// Endpoint mapping — ACTUAL paths determined by Section 0 audit.
// These are DEFAULTS; api.js reads from a config object that the audit populates.
fetchHealthCheck(signal?): Promise<{ok: boolean}>        // GET /api/health
fetchMetrics(signal?): Promise<Metric[]>                 // GET /api/metrics
fetchStatus(signal?): Promise<Status>                    // GET /api/status
fetchPresets(signal?): Promise<Preset[]>                 // GET /api/presets
searchDocuments(query, signal?): Promise<Result[]>       // GET /api/search?q=
fetchHistory(signal?): Promise<HistoryEntry[]>           // GET /api/history
fetchTimeline(docId, signal?): Promise<TimelineEntry[]>  // GET /api/docs/:id/timeline
fetchActions(docId, signal?): Promise<Action[]>          // GET /api/docs/:id/actions
executeAction(docId, actionId, signal?): Promise<void>   // POST /api/docs/:id/actions/:actionId

Error handling:
  class ApiError extends Error {
    constructor(status, message, retryable, endpoint) { ... }
  }
  - All fetchers throw ApiError({ status, message, retryable, endpoint }) on failure.
  - 8-second timeout via AbortController.timeout(8000) — on timeout, throw with retryable: true.
  - On 404: console.error(`Endpoint not found: ${method} ${url} — verify backend route mapping in ROUTES.md`)
  - AbortError handling: if error.name === 'AbortError', the caller returns silently without updating state.
    This is critical for SearchComposer — cancelled requests must not trigger error states.
  - Callers catch and render <ErrorState message onRetry /> (retryable) or <ErrorState message /> (non-retryable).

Health check on Shell mount:
  - Shell calls fetchHealthCheck() before firing screen-specific fetches.
  - If health check fails: full-screen <ErrorState message="Cannot reach server" onRetry /> — no individual skeletons.
  - If health check succeeds: proceed to screen-specific data loading.

Autocomplete debounce: 200ms in SearchComposer. Previous in-flight request cancelled via AbortController before new request fires. AbortError is silently swallowed (see above).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
6. STATE MANAGEMENT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Global state via React Context + hooks (no external dependencies):

ThemeContext:
  State: { mode: "dark"|"light" }
  Actions: TOGGLE_THEME
  Persistence: localStorage key "app-theme", fallback to prefers-color-scheme
  Context value MUST be memoized: useMemo(() => ({ mode, toggle }), [mode])

RouterContext:
  State: { path: string, params: Record<string,string>, query: Record<string,string>, stack: string[] }
  Actions: PUSH, BACK, REPLACE
  Sync: hashchange listener in <Router> dispatches updates
  Context value memoized on path + params + query.

Data fetching — fetch-on-mount pattern (NO stale-while-revalidate in v1):
  Custom hook: useFetch(fetchFn, deps)
    - Calls fetchFn in useEffect with AbortController cleanup on unmount/dep change.
    - Returns { data, loading, error, retry }.
    - No cache. No deduplication. No stale reads. Simple, correct, zero race conditions.
    - Each component owns its own fetch lifecycle.
    - AbortError from cancelled requests is silently ignored (does not set error state).
  Rationale: rolling custom SWR is the #1 source of subtle bugs in custom React data layers.
  For v1, the simplicity of fetch-on-mount with proper cleanup is correct by default.
  v2 consideration: adopt React Query or SWR if cross-screen cache sharing becomes necessary.

Component-local state via useState/useReducer for:
  - Search input value
  - Bottom sheet open/closed
  - Skeleton/loaded/error loading phases (driven by useFetch return values)
  - Form field values in action sheets

Memoization requirements (non-negotiable):
  - React.memo on all list item components: KPIMetric, TimelineItem, ResultCard, HistoryEntry.
  - useCallback on: SearchComposer's debounced onChange handler, TabBar's onNavigate, BottomSheet's onClose.
  - useMemo on: ThemeContext value, RouterContext value, any derived/filtered lists passed as props.
  - Context providers MUST memoize their value object to prevent consumer re-renders on unrelated parent state changes.

Prop drilling banned past 2 levels. Use context or component composition (render props / children) instead.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
7. DESIGN TOKEN SYSTEM (tokens.css)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

/* ── Typography ── */
Font stack: 'Inter', 'SF Pro Display', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif
Font loading: Inter self-hosted as woff2 in src/frontend/web/fonts/inter-{regular,medium,semibold}.woff2
  - <link rel="preload" as="font" type="font/woff2" crossorigin> in <head>
  - font-display: swap

:root {
  /* ── Surfaces (dark default) ── */
  --surface-0: #09090b;           /* App background — zinc-950 */
  --surface-1: #18181b;           /* Card background — zinc-900 */
  --surface-2: #27272a;           /* Elevated card / input — zinc-800 */
  --surface-3: #3f3f46;           /* Hover / pressed — zinc-700 */

  /* ── Text ── */
  --text-primary: #fafafa;        /* zinc-50 */
  --text-secondary: #a1a1aa;      /* zinc-400 */
  --text-tertiary: #71717a;       /* zinc-500 — dark mode: 4.6:1 on surface-0 ✓ */
  --text-inverse: #09090b;        /* For use on accent backgrounds */

  /* ── Brand / Accent ── */
  --accent-default: #a78bfa;      /* violet-400 — confident, premium */
  --accent-hover: #c4b5fd;        /* violet-300 */
  --accent-active: #8b5cf6;       /* violet-500 */
  --accent-subtle: rgba(167, 139, 250, 0.12);

  /* ── Status ── */
  --status-success: #34d399;      /* emerald-400 */
  --status-warning: #fbbf24;      /* amber-400 */
  --status-error: #f87171;        /* red-400 */
  --status-info: #60a5fa;         /* blue-400 */
  --status-pending: #a1a1aa;      /* zinc-400 */

  /* ── Borders ── */
  --border-default: #27272a;      /* zinc-800 */
  --border-subtle: #1e1e22;
  --border-accent: var(--accent-default);

  /* ── Skeleton ── */
  --skeleton-base: var(--surface-1);
  --skeleton-shine: var(--surface-2);

  /* ── Spacing (4px base) ── */
  --space-1: 4px;
  --space-2: 8px;
  --space-3: 12px;
  --space-4: 16px;
  --space-5: 20px;
  --space-6: 24px;
  --space-8: 32px;
  --space-10: 40px;
  --space-12: 48px;
  --space-16: 64px;

  /* ── Typography Scale ── */
  --font-xs: 0.75rem;       /* 12px — line-height: 1rem */
  --font-sm: 0.875rem;      /* 14px — line-height: 1.25rem */
  --font-base: 1rem;        /* 16px — line-height: 1.5rem */
  --font-lg: 1.125rem;      /* 18px — line-height: 1.75rem */
  --font-xl: 1.25rem;       /* 20px — line-height: 1.75rem */
  --font-2xl: 1.375rem;     /* 22px — line-height: 1.75rem — section headers */
  --font-3xl: 1.5rem;       /* 24px — line-height: 2rem */
  --font-4xl: 1.875rem;     /* 30px — line-height: 2.25rem — page titles mobile */
  --font-5xl: 2.25rem;      /* 36px — line-height: 2.5rem — hero display on lg */

  /* ── Fluid Typography ── */
  --font-display: clamp(1.5rem, 1.2rem + 1vw, 1.875rem);   /* Page titles */
  --font-body: clamp(0.875rem, 0.825rem + 0.25vw, 1rem);    /* Body text */

  --lh-tight: 1.2;
  --lh-snug: 1.25;
  --lh-normal: 1.5;
  --lh-relaxed: 1.625;

  /* ── Semantic heading mapping ── */
  /* h1: --font-display (page title)
     h2: --font-3xl / --weight-semibold (section title)
     h3: --font-2xl / --weight-medium (subsection)
     h4: --font-lg / --weight-medium (card title)
     h5: --font-base / --weight-semibold (label)
     h6: --font-sm / --weight-semibold (overline) */

  /* ── Font Weights ── */
  --weight-regular: 400;
  --weight-medium: 500;
  --weight-semibold: 600;

  /* ── Radii ── */
  --radius-sm: 6px;
  --radius-md: 10px;
  --radius-lg: 16px;
  --radius-full: 9999px;

  /* ── Shadows (subtle, no heavy elevation) ── */
  --shadow-sm: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
  --shadow-md: 0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.4);
  --shadow-lg: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);
  --shadow-sheet: 0 -4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);
  --shadow-focus: 0 0 0 2px var(--surface-0), 0 0 0 4px var(--accent-default);

  /* ── Z-index Layers ── */
  --z-base: 0;
  --z-sticky: 100;
  --z-dropdown: 200;
  --z-overlay: 300;
  --z-sheet: 400;
  --z-toast: 500;

  /* ── Motion ── */
  --ease-expo-out: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
  --ease-snap-out: cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1);    /* Confident snap, zero overshoot */
  --ease-smooth: cubic-bezier(0.33, 1, 0.68, 1);
  --ease-snap: cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1);
  --duration-instant: 100ms;
  --duration-fast: 150ms;
  --duration-normal: 300ms;
  --duration-slow: 450ms;

  /* ── Tab Bar ── */
  --tab-active-border: 3px solid var(--accent-default);
}

+  Tab switch history behavior: REPLACE, not PUSH.
+    Tab switches use history.replaceState() — they do NOT create history entries.
+    Only drill-down navigations (Home→Detail, Search→Detail) use history.pushState().
+    This prevents the "history trap": Home → Detail → Search (replace) → Back returns to Detail → Back returns to Home.
+    Correct behavior: Back from a tab always returns to the previous drill-down context, not to another tab.

+  All three tab screens (Home, Search, Library) render simultaneously in the DOM.

+    CSS: display: none;
+    Attribute: inert (prevents keyboard/screen reader interaction with hidden tabs).

+    - Scroll position (each tab's scroll container retains its scrollTop).

+  This is the "render all tabs, show one" pattern. Memory cost is bounded (3 screens, not unbounded).

+Timeout implementation (browser-floor safe):
+  DO NOT use AbortController.timeout() — unavailable on Safari 15 / Chrome 89.
+  Manual timeout pattern used in every fetch function:
+
+    async function fetchWithTimeout(url, options = {}, timeoutMs = 8000) {
+      const controller = new AbortController();
+      const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs);
+      try {
+        const response = await fetch(url, { ...options, signal: controller.signal });
+        return response;
+      } finally {
+        clearTimeout(timeoutId);
+      }
+    }
+
+  If the caller passes its own signal (for cancellation), link it:
+    caller's signal.addEventListener('abort', () => controller.abort());
+  This ensures both timeout AND caller-initiated cancellation work.
+
 Error handling:
   class ApiError extends Error {
-    constructor(status, message, retryable, endpoint) { ... }
+    constructor(status, message, retryable, endpoint) {
+      super(message);
+      this.status = status;
+      this.retryable = retryable;
+      this.endpoint = endpoint;
+    }
   }
   - All fetchers throw ApiError({ status, message, retryable, endpoint }) on failure.
-  - 8-second timeout via AbortController.timeout(8000) — on timeout, throw with retryable: true.
   - On 404: console.error(`Endpoint not found: ${method} ${url} — verify backend route mapping in ROUTES.md`)
   - AbortError handling: if error.name === 'AbortError', the caller returns silently without updating state.
     This is critical for SearchComposer — cancelled requests must not trigger error states.
-  - Callers catch and render <ErrorState message onRetry /> (retryable) or <ErrorState message /> (non-retryable).
-
-Health check on Shell mount:
-  - Shell calls fetchHealthCheck() before firing screen-specific fetches.
-  - If health check fails: full-screen <ErrorState message="Cannot reach server" onRetry /> — no individual skeletons.
-  - If health check succeeds: proceed to screen-specific data loading.
+  - Callers catch and render <ErrorState /> with appropriate type variant.
+
+Automatic retry for retryable errors:
+  - On retryable failure (5xx, timeout), api.js performs ONE automatic retry after 1000ms delay.
+  - If retry also fails, throw the error to the caller for UI handling.
+  - The manual "Retry" button in ErrorState triggers a fresh attempt (not counted against auto-retry).
+  - Non-retryable errors (4xx except 408/429) are thrown immediately, no auto-retry.
+  - 429 Too Many Requests: parse Retry-After header, delay accordingly, then retry once.
+
+Health check on Shell mount (NON-BLOCKING):
+  - Shell calls fetchHealthCheck() on mount.
+  - If health check succeeds: no banner, proceed normally.
+  - If health check fails: show <ConnectivityBanner> at top of shell with message
+    "Server connectivity issue — some features may be unavailable." Banner is dismissible.
+    Screens still mount and attempt their own fetches.
+  - If health check fails AND the first screen-specific fetch also fails: show full-screen
+    <ErrorState type="offline"> with retry. This is the "both failed" threshold.
+  - ConnectivityBanner retries health check every 15 seconds. On success, auto-dismisses.
