-
-  If the caller passes its own signal (for cancellation), link it:
-    caller's signal.addEventListener('abort', () => controller.abort());
-  This ensures both timeout AND caller-initiated cancellation work.
-
-Error handling:
-  class ApiError extends Error {
-    constructor(status, message, retryable, endpoint) {
-      super(message);
-      this.status = status;
-      this.retryable = retryable;
-      this.endpoint = endpoint;
-    }
-  }
-  - All fetchers throw ApiError({ status, message, retryable, endpoint }) on failure.
-  - On 404: console.error(`Endpoint not found: ${method} ${url} — verify backend route mapping in ROUTES.md`)
-  - AbortError handling: if error.name === 'AbortError', the caller returns silently without updating state.
-    This is critical for SearchComposer — cancelled requests must not trigger error states.
-  - Callers catch and render <ErrorState /> with appropriate type variant.
-
-Automatic retry for retryable errors:
-  - On retryable failure (5xx, timeout), api.js performs ONE automatic retry after 1000ms delay.
-  - If retry also fails, throw the error to the caller for UI handling.
-  - The manual "Retry" button in ErrorState triggers a fresh attempt (not counted against auto-retry).
-  - Non-retryable errors (4xx except 408/429) are thrown immediately, no auto-retry.
-  - 429 Too Many Requests: parse Retry-After header, delay accordingly, then retry once.
-
-Health check on Shell mount (NON-BLOCKING):
-  - Shell calls fetchHealthCheck() on mount.
-  - If health check succeeds: no banner, proceed normally.
-  - If health check fails: show <ConnectivityBanner> at top of shell with message
-    "Server connectivity issue — some features may be unavailable." Banner is dismissible.
-    Screens still mount and attempt their own fetches.
-  - If health check fails AND the first screen-specific fetch also fails: show full-screen
-    <ErrorState type="offline"> with retry. This is the "both failed" threshold.
-  - ConnectivityBanner retries health check every 15 seconds. On success, auto-dismisses.
-
-Autocomplete debounce: 200ms in SearchComposer. Previous in-flight request cancelled via AbortController before new request fires. AbortError is silently swallowed (see above).
-
-═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-6. STATE MANAGEMENT
-═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-
-Global state via React Context + hooks (no external dependencies):
-
-ThemeContext:
-  State: { mode: "dark"|"light" }
+│   │   │   │   │   ├── {mountedTabs.has('library') &&
+│   │   │   │   │   │   <div class={activeTab === 'library' ? '' : 'hidden'} inert={activeTab !== 'library'}>
+│   │

objections.description: The artifact is truncated mid-section. Section 6 (State Management) is incomplete — it defines ThemeContext's state shape and stops. Missing: ThemeContext toggle logic, persistence strategy (localStorage key, SSR-safe read), RouterContext full state shape, useFetch hook implementation spec (loading/error/data state machine, stale-while-revalidate policy or not, cache invalidation), and any other contexts. The component tree references ThemeProvider with { mode, toggle } but the spec never defines toggle's behavior (does it persist? does it emit a class on <html>? does it update CSS custom properties via data-theme attribute or class swap?). Without this, four engineers will implement four different state strategies.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete Section 6 with: (1) ThemeContext: full value shape { mode, toggle }, persistence to localStorage under key 'theme-preference', read on mount with fallback to prefers-color-scheme media query, toggle sets data-theme attribute on document.documentElement and persists, useMemo on context value to prevent re-renders. (2) RouterContext: full shape { path, params, query, push, replace, back, isDirectEntry }, state machine for navigation lifecycle. (3) useFetch hook: define the state machine explicitly — idle → loading → success | error, with fields { data, error, isLoading, refetch }, AbortController cleanup on unmount, stale data policy (keep stale data visible during refetch or show skeleton — pick one and document). (4) Document that there are exactly 2 contexts (Theme, Router) and all other state is component-local via useState/useReducer.

objections.location: Section 1: BUILD SYSTEM — esbuild JSX handling
objections.description: esbuild command is missing --jsx=automatic and --jsx-import-source=react flags. Without these, every JSX file needs `import React from 'react'` at the top (the old transform). The file layout uses .jsx extensions and modern React 18, which expects the automatic JSX runtime. If someone writes a component without the React import (which is idiomatic since React 17), esbuild will emit code referencing undefined React.createElement and the app silently fails or throws at runtime.
objections.suggested_fix: Add --jsx=automatic to the esbuild command. This makes esbuild use the jsx-runtime import automatically, matching React 18 conventions. The full command should be: esbuild src/frontend/web/src/main.jsx --bundle --format=esm --outdir=src/frontend/web/dist --minify --target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90 --metafile=meta.json --jsx=automatic

objections.location: Section 4: COMPONENT ARCHITECTURE — Persistent Tab Panels + Detail Screen coexistence
objections.description: The architecture renders all three tab panels simultaneously and conditionally mounts DetailScreen when route is #/doc/:id. But the spec doesn't define what happens to the tab panels when DetailScreen is active. Are all three tab panels set to hidden/inert AND DetailScreen is visible? Is the TabBar still visible on the Detail screen (the spec says Detail has a StickyHeader with back button, implying no tab bar, but the component tree shows TabBar outside the conditional). The Shell renders TabBar unconditionally. On Detail routes, the user sees the bottom tab bar with no tab highlighted (since Detail is not a tab), which is a confusing UX. Or does the tab bar hide? This is architecturally ambiguous.
objections.suggested_fix: Explicitly specify: (1) When route matches #/doc/:id, all three persistent tab panels receive hidden+inert. DetailScreen mounts in the same <main> slot. (2) TabBar is HIDDEN (display:none + inert) on Detail routes — Detail has its own StickyHeader with back navigation. (3) Add a `showTabBar` derived boolean in Shell: `const showTabBar = ['/', '/search', '/library'].includes(currentPath)`. (4) Document this in the component tree with a comment.

objections.location: Section 5: DATA TYPES — Signal linking pattern
objections.description: The signal linking pattern `caller's signal.addEventListener('abort', () => controller.abort())` leaks an event listener if the caller's signal outlives the fetch. When fetchWithTimeout completes normally, the listener remains on the caller's AbortSignal. Over many fetches with the same long-lived signal (e.g., a component-scoped signal reused across refetches), listeners accumulate. This is a slow memory leak.
objections.suggested_fix: Store the listener reference and remove it in the finally block: `const onAbort = () => controller.abort(); callerSignal?.addEventListener('abort', onAbort); try { ... } finally { clearTimeout(timeoutId); callerSignal?.removeEventListener('abort', onAbort); }`. Document this pattern in api.js as the canonical fetch wrapper.

objections.location: Section 3: NAVIGATION — Hash query param parsing edge case
objections.description: The hash parsing `raw.split('?', 2)` correctly limits to 2 parts, but `location.hash.slice(1)` will include any percent-encoded characters. If a search query contains '#' characters (e.g., searching for 'C# programming'), the browser will truncate location.hash at the second '#'. This is a fundamental limitation of hash routing that the spec should acknowledge and handle — either by double-encoding or by documenting it as a known limitation with a workaround.
objections.suggested_fix: Document as a known limitation: 'Hash fragments containing literal # characters are ambiguous per RFC 3986. SearchComposer must encodeURIComponent the query value (which encodes # as %23) before writing to location.hash. Decoding happens in the router via decodeURIComponent on the parsed query params. Add a test case for query strings containing #, &, and = characters.'

objections.location: Section 0: MIGRATION — Missing build step in migration sequence
objections.description: Migration step M3 says 'Build React app → dist/'. But there is no documented npm/package.json setup step. Where does esbuild come from? There's no Step M2.5 that creates package.json, installs react + react-dom + esbuild as dependencies, and adds a build script. An engineer following this migration literally cannot execute M3.
objections.suggested_fix: Insert Step M2.5: 'Create package.json in src/frontend/web/ with dependencies: react@18, react-dom@18, devDependencies: esbuild@0.20+. Add scripts: { build: "esbuild src/main.jsx --bundle ...", dev: "esbuild ... --watch --servedir=.", check-budget: "node scripts/check-bundle-size.js" }. Run npm install. Commit package.json and package-lock.json (NOT node_modules — add to .gitignore).'

objections.location: Section 4: COMPONENT ARCHITECTURE — Missing theme toggle UI
objections.description: The spec defines ThemeProvider with { mode, toggle } and the entire token system supports dark/light switching, but no component in the architecture tree provides the UI affordance to trigger toggle(). There's no theme toggle button in Shell's header, no settings screen, no reference to where the user actually switches themes. The feature exists in state but has no surface.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a ThemeToggle button component to Shell's header area, right-aligned. Spec: 44×44px tap target, sun/moon icon swap with crossfade animation (--duration-fast), aria-label='Switch to {opposite} mode'. Add to file layout as components/theme-toggle.jsx. Add to component tree under <header> in Shell.

objections.location: Section 2: FILE LAYOUT — tokens.css and animations.css placement
objections.description: tokens.css and animations.css are at the root of src/frontend/web/ alongside index.html, but the authored source (.jsx files) is in src/frontend/web/src/. This splits authored assets across two directories. When esbuild bundles from src/, it won't process CSS files outside that directory unless explicitly configured. More importantly, it creates confusion about what's source vs. output — dist/ is output, src/ is source, but the CSS lives in neither.
objections.suggested_fix: Move tokens.css and animations.css to src/frontend/web/src/styles/ and have index.html reference them at their served path. Or, keep them at root (they're global styles loaded by index.html directly, not imported by JS) but add a comment in the file layout documenting WHY: 'Global CSS files are at web/ root because they are linked directly from index.html, not bundled by esbuild. They are not JS module dependencies.'

suggestions.description: Add a `dev` mode esbuild command with --watch and --serve for local development. Without this, every change requires a manual rebuild. The dev command should skip --minify for readable stack traces and add --sourcemap=inline for DevTools debugging. Document: 'npm run dev starts esbuild watch + serves on localhost:3001. Proxy API requests to web_server.py on localhost:8000 via esbuild's --serve proxy option (or document that devs must run web_server.py separately and handle CORS).'

The spec mentions 'high-fidelity skeleton variants with fixed dimensions' but doesn't enumerate which skeleton shapes exist. Define: SkeletonKPI (matches KPIMetric dimensions), SkeletonCard (matches ResultCard/HistoryEntry), SkeletonTimeline (matches TimelineItem), SkeletonText (single line, variable width). Each skeleton must have explicit height/width CSS to prevent CLS, matching the CLS < 0.1 target in Section 1.

searchDocuments and fetchTimeline are specced to return arrays, but the component tree references '20-item pages'. The API contract doesn't define pagination parameters (offset/cursor) or response shape ({ items, hasMore, total? }). Either define the paginated response shape or document that v1 returns all results in a single response and client-side paginates (which changes the API contract significantly).

The spec is thorough on ARIA attributes but doesn't include a verification checklist. Add: 'Before shipping, verify with VoiceOver on Safari and NVDA on Chrome: (1) Skip link moves focus to main content, (2) Tab bar announces current page, (3) Route changes are announced by RouteAnnouncer, (4) Bottom sheet traps focus and announces its title, (5) ErrorState retry buttons are reachable by keyboard, (6) Status card live region announces updates without stealing focus.' This turns accessibility intent into testable acceptance criteria.

objections.description: Mounting all three tab screens simultaneously in DOM with display:none/inert creates a memory bomb on low-end mobile devices. If Home fetches 20 results, Search fetches 20, and Library fetches history, that's 3x JavaScript heap growth with retained component trees, event listeners, and image references. This violates mobile resource constraints and will cause tab crashes on devices with <4GB RAM.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement 'state lifting' pattern instead of DOM persistence. Unmount inactive tabs but preserve their scroll position and search query in a global navigation state store (useNavigationStore). Remount with restored state when tab becomes active. This maintains UX benefits without the memory overhead.

objections.location: Section 3: Bottom Sheet / Section 4: Component Architecture
objections.description: Bottom Sheet specification lacks critical ARIA attributes for modal behavior. Missing role='dialog', aria-modal='true', and aria-labelledby pointing to the sheet title. The inert attribute on main content is insufficient for older screen readers that don't support inert.
objections.suggested_fix: Add role='dialog', aria-modal='true', and aria-labelledby={titleId} to the sheet container. Implement legacy fallback: when sheet opens, add aria-hidden='true' to all siblings of the portal root, not just main and tabbar, to ensure compatibility with VoiceOver iOS 14/15.

objections.description: iOS Safari input zoom prevention is completely absent. Search inputs using default 16px or smaller font-size will trigger automatic page zoom on focus, breaking the app-like experience and causing horizontal overflow. This is a critical mobile UX failure.
objections.suggested_fix: Mandate text-base (16px) minimum on all inputs, search fields, and textareas. Add to tokens.css: input, textarea, select { font-size: 1rem; } with @supports (-webkit-touch-callout: none) { font-size: 16px; } to specifically target iOS. Document that zoom prevention is non-negotiable for v1.

objections.description: No debouncing or throttling specified for RouteAnnouncer. Rapid navigation (accidental double-tap, keyboard power user) will queue multiple aria-live announcements, creating screen reader spam and cognitive overload.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement a 150ms debounce in RouteAnnouncer. If navigation occurs again within 150ms, cancel the previous announcement. Use a single aria-live region with aria-atomic='true' and buffer the text update.

objections.description: 'Visibility-aware polling' is mentioned but not defined. The spec lacks Page Visibility API integration (document.hidden/document.visibilityState) to pause the 30s polling when the tab is backgrounded, wasting battery and CPU.
objections.suggested_fix: Explicitly require: useEffect in StatusCard must listen for visibilitychange events. When document.hidden === true, clearInterval the polling timer. Resume polling immediately when visibility returns to visible, with an immediate fetch if elapsed > 30s.

objections.description: No strategy specified to prevent Flash of Unstyled Content (FOUC) or theme flash on initial load. The inline theme script is mentioned but not its placement strategy or color-scheme meta tag usage.
objections.suggested_fix: Require blocking script in <head> BEFORE any CSS that reads localStorage theme preference and sets data-theme attribute on <html>. Add <meta name='color-scheme' content='dark light'> to prevent system-level dark mode flashes. Inline critical CSS must include the default dark theme variables to prevent paint without tokens.

suggestions.description: Scroll position Map in memory is lost on hard refresh. Consider sessionStorage backup for scroll positions keyed by route path, restoring from storage on initial load if available, then clearing the storage key to prevent stale restoration on future visits.

The manual hash parsing logic is fragile. If the URL contains multiple ? characters (though rare), the split('?', 2) works, but nested encoding might fail. Consider using URLSearchParams on the hash portion after extracting it, or document that hash fragments containing # are forbidden.

Error boundaries catch render crashes but don't log to error reporting. Add componentDidCatch logging to a hypothetical error service or at minimum console.error with component stack to aid debugging in production.

The manual AbortController timeout pattern is verbose. Consider extracting to a useApiHook that handles timeout, retry logic, and cleanup on unmount automatically, rather than repeating in every fetch function.

objections.description: Design token system is referenced throughout but no actual token values are defined. No color palette, spacing scale, or typography scale specified. Implementation cannot proceed without concrete token values.
objections.suggested_fix: Add Section 7: Design Token System with explicit CSS custom property definitions for colors (--surface-0 through --surface-4, --text-primary/secondary/tertiary, --accent-default/hover/active), spacing (--space-1 through --space-12), typography (--font-size-xs through --font-size-2xl, --line-height-tight/normal/relaxed), and radii (--radius-sm/md/lg/full).

objections.description: ThemeContext state defined but no dark/light theme color mappings specified. Spec says 'dark default + light override' in tokens.css but provides zero actual color values. This violates the convergence criteria requiring dark mode on every screen.
objections.suggested_fix: Define complete color palette for both themes with WCAG AA contrast ratios (4.5:1 minimum for normal text, 3:1 for large text). Include semantic color mappings for success/warning/error states in both light and dark modes.

objections.description: Motion system mentioned but no actual animation durations, easing curves, or keyframe definitions provided. Spec bans 'ease/linear timing' but doesn't specify what timing functions TO use. Cannot implement consistent motion without this.
objections.suggested_fix: Add motion token definitions: --ease-out-expo, --ease-out-quart, --duration-fast (150ms), --duration-normal (300ms), --duration-slow (500ms). Include @keyframes for skeleton shimmer, bottom-sheet slide-up, fade-in transitions.

objections.description: Focus visible states mentioned (':focus-visible styles apply') but no actual focus ring specifications. Premium/confident aesthetic requires precise focus ring color, width, and offset values.
objections.suggested_fix: Specify focus ring tokens: --focus-ring-color (accent with 40% opacity), --focus-ring-width (2px), --focus-ring-offset (2px). Include reduced-motion alternative (no ring animation, instant appearance).

objections.description: Colorblind support mentioned (shape variants: circle/triangle/square) but no mapping defined for which shape corresponds to which state. Implementation will be inconsistent without this.
objections.suggested_fix: Define shape-to-state mapping: circle=success/info, triangle=warning/pending, square=error. Document in icons/README.md with SVG path specifications for each shape variant.

objections.description: Tab bar active state described ('accent-colored filled icon') but no actual accent color value defined. Inactive state ('--text-tertiary') also has no defined value.
objections.suggested_fix: Add tab bar visual specs to token system: --tab-active-color, --tab-inactive-color, --tab-border-width (4px), --tab-icon-size (24px). Include hover/active states for pointer devices.

objections.description: Skeleton placeholders mentioned as 'high-fidelity' but no visual specification for shimmer animation direction, gradient stops, or animation duration.
objections.suggested_fix: Define skeleton animation: linear gradient from --surface-2 to --surface-1 to --surface-2, animated left-to-right over 1.5s infinite loop. Match exact dimensions of final content to prevent CLS.

suggestions.description: Add CSS custom property documentation comments explaining when to use each token (e.g., --text-tertiary for secondary labels, not body copy).
Include @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) overrides that disable all non-essential animations while preserving state changes.
Add visual regression testing strategy using Playwright screenshot comparisons for key screens in both light and dark modes.

objections.description: Reviewer did not return valid JSON
objections.suggested_fix: Return a valid JSON review object

orchestrator_response.content:
Production Frontend Implementation Spec — v5 (Convergence Round 5)

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
0. PRE-MIGRATION AUDIT & BACKEND ENDPOINT MAPPING
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Before any frontend code is written, execute this checklist:

Step 1: Audit web_server.py routes.
  - List every route handler in web_server.py.
  - Map each existing endpoint to the api.js fetch function it will serve.
  - If backend uses /api/v1/search, api.js calls /api/v1/search — the frontend adapts to the backend, never the reverse.
  - Document the exact mapping in a ROUTES.md file at repo root.

Step 2: Confirm static-file serving behavior.
  - Verify web_server.py serves index.html for GET / with no path-based routing for sub-pages.
  - Confirm it serves static assets from src/frontend/web/ (CSS, JS, fonts, images).
  - If web_server.py has catch-all or path-based HTML serving, document it — this affects routing strategy.

Step 3: Verify API health endpoint exists.
  - If GET /api/health (or equivalent) exists, map it. If not, flag for backend team to add.
  - api.js uses this on Shell mount to display connectivity warnings (non-blocking — see Section 5).

Step 4: Runtime 404 detection — distinguish dev errors from data 404s.
  - api.js must log a clear console.error with the endpoint path when it receives a 404.
  - API-route 404 message: "Endpoint not found: GET /api/foo — verify backend route mapping in ROUTES.md"
  - Resource 404 (e.g., /api/docs/:id where :id doesn't exist): render <ErrorState type="not-found" message="This item may have been deleted" /> — no retry button.
  - Dev-error 404 (endpoint itself missing): render <ErrorState type="endpoint-missing" message="Feature unavailable" onRetry /> — retryable.
  - Distinguish by convention: if the route pattern matches a known endpoint template but the specific resource ID fails, it's a resource 404. If the route pattern itself is unknown, it's a dev error.

Step 5: Verify Content-Type headers.
  - Confirm web_server.py serves .js files with Content-Type: application/javascript (not text/plain).
  - Confirm .css files with Content-Type: text/css.
  - Confirm .woff2 files with Content-Type: font/woff2.
  - Some Python HTTP servers (http.server, SimpleHTTPServer) serve incorrect MIME types.
    Browsers refuse ES module execution with strict MIME type errors — this is a silent, hard-to-diagnose failure.
  - If MIME types are wrong, fix in web_server.py before proceeding.

Migration sequence (big-bang replacement — no feature flags):
  Step M1: Copy current index.html → _legacy/index-alpine.html (preservation, not coexistence).
  Step M2: Create new index.html with React mount point, theme script, CSS links.
  Step M2.5: Create package.json in src/frontend/web/ with:
    dependencies: react@18, react-dom@18
    devDependencies: esbuild@0.20+
    scripts:
      "build": "esbuild src/main.jsx --bundle --format=esm --outdir=dist --minify --target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90 --metafile=meta.json --jsx=automatic"
      "dev": "esbuild src/main.jsx --bundle --format=esm --outdir=dist --target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90 --jsx=automatic --sourcemap=inline --watch"
      "check-budget": "node scripts/check-bundle-size.js"
    Run npm install. Commit package.json and package-lock.json.
    Add node_modules/ to .gitignore.
  Step M3: Run npm run build → dist/main.js. web_server.py serves new index.html at / unchanged.
  Step M3.5: Verify MIME types (Step 5 above) by loading dist/main.js in browser — check DevTools Network tab for Content-Type and console for MIME errors.
  Step M4: Smoke-test all API routes from new frontend against running backend.
  Step M5: Remove _legacy/ after 2 weeks of production stability.
  Rollback: revert index.html to _legacy/index-alpine.html. Zero backend changes required.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1. BUILD SYSTEM & MODULE STRATEGY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Build tool: esbuild (single dependency, zero-config).

Decision: esbuild bundles React into the output. No vendor/ directory. No import maps.
Rationale: import maps require Safari 16.4+ / Firefox 108+ minimum. Bundling React eliminates
the compatibility risk entirely and simplifies the build to a single command. The bundle size
difference is zero since React ends up in the output either way.

Production command:
  esbuild src/frontend/web/src/main.jsx \
    --bundle \
    --format=esm \
    --outdir=src/frontend/web/dist \
    --minify \
    --target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90 \
    --metafile=meta.json \
    --jsx=automatic

Development command:
  esbuild src/frontend/web/src/main.jsx \
    --bundle \
    --format=esm \
    --outdir=src/frontend/web/dist \
    --target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90 \
    --jsx=automatic \
    --sourcemap=inline \
    --watch

  Dev workflow: run `npm run dev` in one terminal (esbuild watch rebuilds on file change).
  Run web_server.py in another terminal. Both serve from the same directory.
  No proxy needed — esbuild writes to dist/, web_server.py serves the whole web/ directory.
  API requests go to web_server.py directly (same origin, no CORS).

--jsx=automatic rationale: enables React 18's automatic JSX runtime. Components do NOT need
`import React from 'react'` at the top. esbuild injects jsx-runtime imports automatically.
Without this flag, any component missing the React import silently fails at runtime.

Target rationale: using explicit browser targets instead of es2020 lets esbuild make
optimal per-browser syntax decisions. This is self-documenting of the actual support matrix.

IMPORTANT: esbuild --target downlevels SYNTAX only, not runtime APIs.
Banned APIs (not available on browser floor):
  - AbortController.timeout() — Safari 16+ only
  - Array.prototype.at() — Safari 15.4+ only (borderline, avoid)
  - Object.hasOwn() — Safari 15.4+ only
  - structuredClone() — Safari 15.4+ only
  - crypto.randomUUID() — Safari 15.4+ only
  Add this as a lint rule or PR checklist item. Use Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call() etc.

CSS strategy: tokens.css and animations.css are NOT imported by JS. They are standalone
<link> references in index.html, loaded directly by the browser. esbuild does not process them.
This is intentional — global design tokens and keyframes are authored CSS, not component CSS.

No --splitting in v1 (splitting requires import maps for shared chunks in browsers;
we just eliminated import maps). Single entry bundle. Code-split in v2 via dynamic import()
if bundle exceeds budget.

Bundle budget enforcement:
  CI step parses meta.json, fails build if dist/main.js gzipped > 80KB.
  Budget breakdown (estimated):
    React 18 + ReactDOM:  ~42KB gzipped
    App components:       ~15KB gzipped
    Icons (tree-shaken):  ~3KB gzipped
    CSS (tokens + anim):  ~4KB gzipped (loaded separately, not in JS budget)
    Total JS:             ~60KB gzipped (20KB headroom to 80KB cap)

Preact escape hatch: if bundle exceeds 80KB, switch via esbuild alias:
  --alias:react=preact/compat --alias:react-dom=preact/compat
  Preact compat is ~4KB vs React's 42KB. Document this as v1.1 option, not default.

Linting (non-negotiable for v1):
  devDependencies: eslint, eslint-plugin-react-hooks
  .eslintrc: { "plugins": ["react-hooks"], "rules": { "react-hooks/rules-of-hooks": "error", "react-hooks/exhaustive-deps": "warn" } }
  CI runs eslint before build. This catches stale closures and missing dependencies.

Core Web Vitals targets (non-negotiable):
  LCP: < 2.5s on 3G throttled
  FID / INP: < 100ms
  CLS: < 0.1
  Implementation requirements to hit these:
    - index.html inlines critical CSS for above-fold content (shell chrome, skeleton placeholders).
    - tokens.css and animations.css loaded via <link rel="stylesheet"> (not @import — avoids waterfall).
    - Fonts loaded via <link rel="preload" as="font" type="font/woff2" crossorigin> with font-display: swap.
    - All above-fold layout uses explicit dimensions (no layout shift from late-loading content).
    - Skeleton placeholders match final content dimensions to prevent CLS on data arrival.

Source code lives in src/frontend/web/src/ (JSX, full React idioms).
Built output lands in src/frontend/web/dist/ (static ES module, minified, tree-shaken).
index.html references dist/main.js via <script type="module">.
web_server.py serves src/frontend/web/ unchanged — only static file additions.

Browser support floor: Chrome 89+, Safari 15+, Firefox 90+, Edge 89+.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
2. FILE LAYOUT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

src/frontend/web/
├── index.html                          # Shell HTML, blocking theme script, CSS links, inline critical CSS
├── tokens.css                          # Design tokens (dark default + light override) + @font-face + iOS zoom fix
├── animations.css                      # Keyframes + motion utility classes + reduced-motion overrides
├── package.json                        # react, react-dom, esbuild — see Section 0 M2.5
├── package-lock.json
├── .eslintrc.json                      # react-hooks plugin, exhaustive-deps
├── scripts/
│   └── check-bundle-size.js            # Parses meta.json, fails if gzip > 80KB
├── fonts/
│   ├── inter-regular.woff2
│   ├── inter-medium.woff2
│   └── inter-semibold.woff2
├── src/                                # SOURCE (JSX, authored code)
│   ├── main.jsx                        # Entry: mounts <App/>
│   ├── app.jsx                         # App root: ThemeProvider → Router → Shell
│   ├── state/
│   │   ├── theme.jsx                   # ThemeContext + useTheme hook + persistence
│   │   ├── navigation.jsx              # RouterContext + useRoute, hash-based routing
│   │   └── use-fetch.js               # useFetch hook: fetch-on-mount + AbortController cleanup
│   ├── api.js                          # All fetch functions, manual timeout, error handling, retry
│   ├── utils.js                        # cx() class merger, debounce, format helpers, idle-callback wrapper
│   ├── icons/                          # One file per icon, Phosphor-style, size-specific stroke widths
│   │   ├── home.jsx
│   │   ├── search.jsx
│   │   ├── library.jsx
│   │   ├── chevron-left.jsx
│   │   ├── more-horizontal.jsx
│   │   ├── status-dot.jsx              # Shape variants: circle=success|info, triangle=warning|pending, square=error
│   │   ├── alert-circle.jsx
│   │   ├── sun.jsx                     # Theme toggle: light mode icon
│   │   ├── moon.jsx                    # Theme toggle: dark mode icon
│   │   └── index.js                    # Barrel re-export (tree-shaken by esbuild)
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── shell.jsx                   # Main layout: skip-link + header + theme-toggle + content + tab bar
│   │   ├── skip-link.jsx               # WCAG 2.4.1 skip navigation link
│   │   ├── tab-bar.jsx                 # Bottom nav (role=navigation, aria-current=page)
│   │   ├── theme-toggle.jsx            # Sun/moon icon button, 44×44px, aria-label, crossfade
│   │   ├── connectivity-banner.jsx     # Non-blocking health check warning banner
│   │   ├── bottom-sheet.jsx            # Draggable sheet w/ focus trap + portal + role=dialog
│   │   ├── focus-trap.jsx              # Generic focus trap utility
│   │   ├── portal.jsx                  # React portal to document.body
│   │   ├── skeleton.jsx                # Skeleton variants: KPI, Card, Timeline, Text — fixed dimensions
│   │   ├── status-card.jsx             # Live status with visibility-aware polling
│   │   ├── kpi-metric.jsx              # Single KPI display (React.memo with comparator)
│   │   ├── timeline-item.jsx           # Single timeline row (React.memo with comparator)
│   │   ├── result-card.jsx             # Single search result (React.memo with comparator)
│   │   ├── history-entry.jsx           # Single history row (React.memo with comparator)
│   │   ├── search-composer.jsx         # Search input + preset chips
│   │   ├── empty-state.jsx             # Empty/zero-data illustration
│   │   ├── error-state.jsx             # API failure w/ retry action — supports type variants
│   │   ├── error-boundary.jsx          # React error boundary (catches render crashes, logs to console.error)
│   │   └── route-announcer.jsx         # aria-live region for SPA navigation, 150ms debounce
│   └── screens/
│       ├── home.jsx                    # Dashboard / Home tab (wrapped in ErrorBoundary)
│       ├── search.jsx                  # Search tab (wrapped in ErrorBoundary)
│       ├── library.jsx                 # Library/History tab (wrapped in ErrorBoundary)
│       └── detail.jsx                  # Drill-down timeline view (wrapped in ErrorBoundary)
├── dist/                               # BUILD OUTPUT (gitignored)
│   └── main.js
└── _legacy/                            # Quarantined old Alpine files (removed after 2 weeks)
    └── index-alpine.html

Note on CSS file placement: tokens.css and animations.css are at web/ root (alongside
index.html) because they are linked directly from index.html via <link>, NOT bundled by
esbuild. They are not JS module dependencies. esbuild processes only the src/ tree.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3. NAVIGATION & INFORMATION ARCHITECTURE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Bottom tab bar: 3 tabs (Home, Search, Library).
  - Tabs are parallel peer destinations. No drill-down views in tabs.
  - Semantic markup: <nav aria-label="Main"> with <a> elements, NOT role="tablist".
    Rationale: these are route-level navigations that change the URL, not panel switches.
    Active link gets aria-current="page". Inactive links have no aria-current.
  - Active tab visual: accent-colored filled icon + label text + 4px bottom border (--accent-default).
    Inactive: outline-stroke icon + --text-tertiary label. Both color AND weight differentiation required.
  - Tab bar icon size: 24px. Label font: --font-size-xs (12px). Tab item height: 56px + safe area.
  - Pointer device hover state: --surface-2 background on tab item (subtle, not accent).
  - Tab bar receives `inert` attribute + `aria-hidden="true"` when bottom sheet is open.
    CSS: `[inert] { visibility: hidden; }` (prevents screen reader and keyboard access).
    Main content area ALSO receives `inert` + `aria-hidden="true"` when bottom sheet is open.
    Focus returns to the trigger element that opened the sheet on close.
  - Tab bar is HIDDEN on Detail routes: display:none + inert.
    Shell derives: `const showTabBar = ['/', '/search', '/library'].includes(currentPath)`
    Detail screen has its own StickyHeader with back navigation — no tab bar needed.

  Tab switch history behavior: REPLACE, not PUSH.
    Tab switches use history.replaceState() — they do NOT create history entries.
    Only drill-down navigations (Home→Detail, Search→Detail) use history.pushState().
    This prevents the "history trap": Home → Detail → Search (replace) → Back returns to Detail → Back returns to Home.
    Correct behavior: Back from a tab always returns to the previous drill-down context, not to another tab.

  iOS safe area: Tab bar applies padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px).
    index.html includes <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1, viewport-fit=cover">.
    --safe-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px) defined in tokens.css.

Drill-down views (Detail/Timeline): accessed from tapping items on Home or Search results.
  - When route matches #/doc/:id: all three persistent tab panels receive hidden+inert.
    DetailScreen mounts in the same <main> slot. Tab bar is hidden (see above).
  - Back affordance: chevron-left button in top-left header area (44×44px tap target).
  - Push onto history via pushState (not replaceState).
  - Deep link handling: when DetailScreen mounts and detects direct entry (no history.state
    or the router's internal stack is empty), the back button renders as a Home link (href="#/")
    instead of calling history.back(). This prevents stranding users at a dead-end.

Routing strategy: hash-based (#/) for zero-config static serving.
  Confirmed: web_server.py is a static file server. It does NOT serve route-specific HTML.
  Hash routing is an intentional v1 decision.
  v2 escape hatch: migrate to History API pushState + web_server.py catch-all that serves
  index.html for all non-API, non-static paths. Document in ARCHITECTURE.md.

  CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION NOTE — Hash query param parsing:
    Hash query params are NOT accessible via location.search. The browser sees everything
    after # as the fragment. The Router MUST parse location.hash manually:

      function parseHash(hash) {
        const raw = hash.slice(1) || '/';
        const [path, queryString] = raw.split('?');
        const query = queryString
          ? Object.fromEntries(new URLSearchParams(queryString))
          : {};
        return { path: path || '/', query };
      }

    If someone implements useRoute() by reading new URL(location).searchParams, every
    preset-initiated search will silently fail with an empty query.

    Edge cases to test:
      '#'           → { path: '/', query: {} }
      '#/'          → { path: '/', query: {} }
      '#/search'    → { path: '/search', query: {} }
      '#/search?q=test'        → { path: '/search', query: { q: 'test' } }
      '#/search?q=test&page=1' → { path: '/search', query: { q: 'test', page: '1' } }
      '#/search?q=C%23+prog'   → { path: '/search', query: { q: 'C# prog' } }

    Known limitation — hash fragments containing literal # characters:
      Hash fragments with literal # are ambiguous per RFC 3986. The browser truncates
      location.hash at the second #. SearchComposer MUST encodeURIComponent the query
      value before writing to location.hash (encodes # as %23). Decoding happens
      automatically via URLSearchParams. Add a test case for queries containing #, &, = chars.

  Routes:
    #/              → Home screen
    #/search        → Search screen (reads ?q= param for preset-initiated searches)
    #/library       → Library screen
    #/doc/:id       → Detail/Timeline screen (drill-down, not a tab)

  Base path variable: const BASE = '' (empty string). If app moves to /app/ subpath,
  change to '/app' and all route matching prepends BASE. Defined once in navigation.jsx.

  state/navigation.jsx exports:
    RouterContext: { path, params, query, push(path), replace(path), back(), isDirectEntry }
    useRoute(): reads context
    <Router>: listens to hashchange, parses hash path + query params (using parseHash above),
      updates context, announces to route-announcer, manages scroll restoration

  Focus management on route change:
    - Each screen's root container has tabindex="-1" (enables programmatic focus).
    - On route change, focus moves to the screen container element.
    - :focus-visible styles apply; :focus:not(:focus-visible) gets outline: none (no ugly ring on mouse click).
    - RouteAnnouncer speaks "Navigated to {page name}" via aria-live="assertive".

  RouteAnnouncer debounce:
    - 150ms debounce on announcement updates. If navigation occurs again within 150ms,
      cancel the previous announcement.
    - Single aria-live region with aria-atomic="true".
    - Implementation: useEffect with setTimeout(150ms), clear on cleanup or re-fire.

  Scroll restoration:
    - On pushState (drill-down): store window.scrollY in a bounded LRU Map keyed by outgoing route path.
    - LRU Map: max 20 entries. On insert at capacity, delete the oldest (first) key.
    - On back navigation: restore scrollY from the Map after target screen renders (requestAnimationFrame).
    - On replaceState (tab switch): scroll to 0 (tabs manage their own scroll internally).
    - The Map lives in memory. Scroll positions are transient. Deliberately NOT sessionStorage —
      stale scroll positions after data changes cause worse UX than resetting to top.

Search presets on Home: tapping a preset navigates to #/search?q={encodeURIComponent(preset.query)}.
  SearchScreen reads ?q= from the hash query on mount and auto-executes the search.
  The Search tab becomes visually active. The preset is a shortcut, not a duplicate search surface.

  Manual search URL sync: SearchScreen uses history.replaceState (never pushState) to update
  #/search?q={encodeURIComponent(value)} as the user types, debounced at 300ms. This ensures:
    - Search is shareable/bookmarkable at any point.
    - Refresh preserves the current query.
    - History is NOT polluted with per-keystroke entries.

Lazy-Mount Tab Panels (state preservation without DOM persistence):
  DECISION: Lazy mount + state caching, NOT simultaneous DOM mount.
  Rationale: mounting all 3 tabs simultaneously fires 3x API calls on app init, retains 3x
  component trees in memory, and wastes resources on screens the user may never visit.
  Low-end mobile devices (<4GB RAM) will suffer.

  Implementation:
    - Each tab screen mounts on FIRST VISIT, not on app init.
    - Once mounted, the tab stays mounted for the session (display:none + inert when inactive).
    - A tab that has NEVER been visited does not exist in the DOM.
    - Shell tracks: `const [mountedTabs, setMountedTabs] = useState(new Set(['home']))`.
      Home is pre-mounted (it's the default route). Search and Library mount on first navigation.
    - On tab switch: add tab to mountedTabs Set if not present, then toggle visibility.
    - Each tab's scroll container retains its scrollTop because the DOM node persists.
    - API calls fire only when the tab mounts for the first time.

    v1.1 enhancement (document, do not implement): if profiling shows memory pressure,
    add a keepalive timeout — unmount a tab if user hasn't visited it in 10 minutes,
    with state snapshot to useRef before unmount.

  When route is #/doc/:id (Detail):
    - All mounted tab panels receive hidden+inert.
    - DetailScreen mounts in the same <main> slot.
    - Tab bar hidden (showTabBar = false).
    - On back navigation, the previous tab's panel becomes visible again (already mounted).

Bottom sheet:
  - Invoked from contextual buttons (not a tab destination).
  - Container attributes: role="dialog", aria-modal="true", aria-labelledby={titleId}.
  - Opens over content with overlay (--surface-0 at 60% opacity).
  - Drag handle: 44×44px interactive area containing a 32px wide × 4px tall visual indicator,
    --surface-3 background, --radius-full, centered with var(--space-4) top margin.
    The 44×44px area is the touch target; the 4×32px bar is the visual affordance.
  - Dismiss: drag down past 20% of sheet height OR downward velocity > 0.5px/ms, Escape key, overlay tap.
  - Focus trapped inside while open (FocusTrap component).
  - When sheet opens: TabBar AND <main> receive `inert` + `aria-hidden="true"`.
    Legacy fallback: also add aria-hidden="true" to all direct children of <body> except
    the portal root. This ensures VoiceOver on iOS 14/15 (which doesn't support inert) correctly
    limits its navigation scope.
  - On close: remove all inert/aria-hidden attributes. Check triggerRef.current?.isConnected
    before returning focus. If trigger is disconnected, fall back to
    document.querySelector('[role="main"]')?.focus() or the first focusable element in viewport.

Quick actions on list items: explicit "⋯" (more) icon button (44×44px tap target). No long-press. No swipe gestures in v1.

Pull-to-refresh: deferred to v2. v1 uses manual refresh via error-state retry buttons and
status card's automatic polling. overscroll-behavior-y: contain applied to main scroll container
to prevent browser default pull-to-refresh (which triggers full page reload and is worse than no PTR).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
4. COMPONENT ARCHITECTURE MAP
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

<App>
├── <ThemeProvider value={{ mode, toggle }}>        // useMemo on value object
│   ├── <Router>
│   │   ├── <RouteAnnouncer />                     // aria-live="assertive", aria-atomic="true", sr-only, 150ms debounce
│   │   ├── <ErrorBoundary fallback={<ErrorState type="crash" />}>   // last-resort app-level
│   │   │   ├── <Shell>
│   │   │   │   ├── <SkipLink target="#main-content" />  // WCAG 2.4.1 — first focusable element
│   │   │   │   ├── <ConnectivityBanner />               // Non-blocking health warning, 15s retry interval
│   │   │   │   ├── <header>                             // Sticky top bar

│   │   │   │   │   // mountedTabs: Set — starts with {'home'}, grows on navigation

│   │   │   │   │   │   └── <RecentResults /> | <EmptyState /> | <ErrorState onRetry />
│   │   │   │   │   │   </ErrorBoundary>

│   │   │   │   │   │

│   │   │   │   │   │   <div class={activeTab === 'search' ? '' : 'hidden'} inert={activeTab !== 'search'}>
│   │   │   │   │   │   <ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │   │   <SearchScreen tabindex="-1">
│   │   │   │   │   │   ├── <SearchComposer />              // useCallback, 200ms debounce, AbortController
│   │   │   │   │   │   └── <ResultsList>                   // paginated, 20-item pages
│   │   │   │   │   │       └── <ResultCard /> ×N           // React.memo

│   │   │   │   │   │   </ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │   │   </div>}
│   │   │   │   │   │
