objections.location: Section 4 — COMPONENT ARCHITECTURE MAP (bottom of artifact)
objections.description: The artifact is truncated mid-sentence inside the Library tab panel definition. Sections 5 through the end (presumably covering: detail screen tree, api.js implementation, useFetch hook, design token values, animation/motion system, dark/light theme token definitions, skeleton dimension specs, accessibility audit checklist, and the remaining 3 key screens) are entirely missing. This is not a minor omission — the convergence criteria explicitly require 'at least 3 key screens', 'motion system', 'dark/light theme switching', and 'design token system' to be fully specified before any agent can APPROVE. The spec currently delivers ~60% of the required deliverables.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the artifact in full. At minimum, the following sections must exist with implementation-ready detail: (1) Remainder of Section 4 — Library tab panel, Detail screen component tree, BottomSheet tree. (2) Section 5 — Design Token System with actual HSL values for every token in both dark and light modes. (3) Section 6 — Motion System with exact keyframe definitions, timing functions (cubic-bezier values), durations, and reduced-motion overrides. (4) Section 7 — api.js specification (timeout handling, retry logic, response parsing, error classification). (5) Section 8 — useFetch hook contract (loading/error/data states, AbortController lifecycle, stale-while-revalidate or not). (6) Section 9 — Screen-by-screen layout specifications for Home, Search/Detail, and Library with exact spacing, responsive breakpoint behaviors, and skeleton-to-content transition rules. (7) Section 10 — Accessibility compliance matrix (WCAG 2.1 AA checklist mapped to components).

objections.description: The spec uses `inert` as a JSX attribute on React elements (e.g., `inert={activeTab !== 'home'}`). React 18 does NOT forward the `inert` attribute to the DOM — it silently drops unknown boolean HTML attributes that aren't in its allowlist. This was only fixed in React 19. On React 18, every inactive tab panel will remain keyboard-navigable and screen-reader-visible, completely breaking the lazy-mount isolation model. This is a correctness bug that will ship silently.
objections.suggested_fix: Use a ref-based approach: `useEffect(() => { if (ref.current) { if (isInactive) ref.current.setAttribute('inert', ''); else ref.current.removeAttribute('inert'); } }, [isInactive])`. Apply the same pattern to the bottom sheet's inert management of <main> and <TabBar>. Document this as a known React 18 limitation in the spec with the exact workaround code. Alternatively, add a thin `<InertContainer active={boolean}>` wrapper component that encapsulates the ref logic.

objections.description: The build command references `src/main.jsx` as the entrypoint in package.json scripts, but the actual file lives at `src/frontend/web/src/main.jsx`. The esbuild CLI command in Section 1 uses the full path, but the npm script in M2.5 uses a relative path. Since package.json lives in `src/frontend/web/`, the correct entrypoint is `src/main.jsx` relative to that directory — BUT the `--outdir=dist` is also relative, which is correct. However, the production command in Section 1 uses absolute repo-relative paths (`src/frontend/web/src/main.jsx`, `src/frontend/web/dist`). These two invocation patterns are inconsistent and will cause CI/CD confusion about working directory expectations.
objections.suggested_fix: Standardize: package.json scripts use paths relative to `src/frontend/web/` (where package.json lives). The Section 1 'production command' and 'development command' blocks should explicitly state `cd src/frontend/web && npx esbuild src/main.jsx ...` or simply reference `npm run build` from that directory. Remove the absolute-path versions or label them clearly as 'if running from repo root'.

objections.description: React's useState does NOT trigger re-renders when you mutate a Set in place. The implementation note says `add tab to mountedTabs Set if not present, then toggle visibility` but doesn't specify the immutable update pattern. A naive `mountedTabs.add('search')` won't re-render. This will manifest as: user taps Search tab, nothing mounts, no error thrown. Silent failure.
objections.suggested_fix: Specify the exact state update pattern: `setMountedTabs(prev => prev.has(tabId) ? prev : new Set([...prev, tabId]))`. This creates a new Set reference, triggering the re-render. Alternatively, use an object `{ home: true, search: false, library: false }` which is more idiomatic for React state and avoids the Set serialization footgun entirely.

objections.description: The spec mentions 'visibility-aware 30s poll' for StatusCard and '15s retry interval' for ConnectivityBanner but never defines the visibility-awareness implementation. Is this Page Visibility API? IntersectionObserver? Both? What happens when the tab is backgrounded — does polling stop? What happens on tab-panel hide (display:none + inert) — does a StatusCard on the Home tab keep polling when the user switches to Search? display:none elements are NOT reported as invisible by IntersectionObserver (they're removed from the rendering tree entirely, so IO never fires for them). This needs an explicit implementation contract.
objections.suggested_fix: Define: (1) Use Page Visibility API (`document.visibilitychange`) to pause ALL polling when browser tab is backgrounded. (2) Use a custom `useTabActive(tabId)` hook that reads from Shell's active tab state to pause polling when the React tab panel is hidden. IntersectionObserver is the wrong tool here because display:none bypasses it. (3) On resume: do NOT immediately fire — wait for the next interval tick. This prevents thundering herd on tab-foreground.

objections.description: The spec specifies Inter in 3 weights (regular, medium, semibold) as .woff2 files. Inter Regular is ~96KB, Medium ~97KB, Semibold ~98KB uncompressed (~38-40KB each gzipped). That's ~115KB gzipped for fonts alone. The CSS budget is listed as ~4KB but the font budget is completely absent. Fonts are the single largest asset category and will dominate LCP on 3G. The spec's LCP target of <2.5s on 3G is unachievable with 3 unconstrained font files.
objections.suggested_fix: Subset the fonts. Inter full Latin is ~96KB per weight; subsetting to Basic Latin + Latin-1 Supplement (U+0000-00FF) brings each weight to ~15-18KB (~6-7KB gzipped). Add a font subsetting step to the build: `pyftsubset inter-regular.woff2 --unicodes='U+0000-00FF,U+2010-2027' --flavor=woff2`. Add font budget to the budget table: ≤25KB gzipped total for all 3 weights. If subsetting tooling is out of scope for v1, reduce to 2 weights (regular + semibold — drop medium) and use font-weight numeric values to fake medium via font-synthesis.

objections.location: Section 3 — Bottom sheet, focus trap + portal
objections.description: The spec says bottom sheet uses a portal to document.body, and the focus trap is inside the sheet. But the `inert` management strategy says 'TabBar AND <main> receive inert'. If the portal renders OUTSIDE <main> (which it does — it's on document.body), and you're setting inert on <main>, the portal content is NOT inside an inert subtree, which is correct. But the legacy fallback says 'add aria-hidden to all direct children of <body> except the portal root'. This requires knowing the portal root's DOM identity at the point where you're iterating body children. The spec doesn't define the portal root element's ID or how the inert-management code identifies it.
objections.suggested_fix:
Define: index.html includes `
` as a sibling to `
`. Portal component renders into #portal-root. Inert management iterates `document.body.children`, skips `#portal-root`, sets aria-hidden on everything else. This is deterministic and doesn't require runtime portal detection.

objections.description: The spec says tab switches use `history.replaceState()` to avoid history pollution. But with hash-based routing, the actual navigation is changing `location.hash`. Directly setting `location.hash = '#/search'` triggers a hashchange event AND pushes a history entry. You MUST use `history.replaceState(null, '', '#/search')` explicitly — setting location.hash is NOT equivalent. The spec should call this out explicitly to prevent the most common implementation mistake.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a boxed warning in the navigation.jsx contract: 'NEVER set location.hash directly for tab switches. Always use history.replaceState(state, "", "#" + path). Setting location.hash pushes to history stack, defeating the replace semantics.' Add this as a code comment in the navigate/replace function.

suggestions.description: Add a Step M0: run the existing Alpine/Tailwind index.html and screenshot or list all visible features/interactions. This becomes the regression checklist for the new implementation. Without it, there's no way to verify feature parity after migration. A 10-minute manual inventory prevents 10 hours of 'wait, didn't the old version do X?'

Add eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y to devDependencies. The spec is extremely thorough on accessibility requirements, but none of them are enforced at lint time. jsx-a11y catches missing aria-labels, invalid roles, missing alt text, and non-interactive element event handlers at build time. This is the cheapest accessibility enforcement available.

ErrorBoundary must be a class component (React 18 has no hook equivalent for componentDidCatch / getDerivedStateFromError). The file layout lists it alongside functional components with no callout. Add a comment in the file layout: 'Class component — React requirement for error boundaries. No hooks equivalent exists.' This prevents a future contributor from 'modernizing' it into a broken functional component.

SearchComposer input debounce is specified as 200ms in Section 4's component tree comment, but URL sync debounce is 300ms in Section 3's 'Manual search URL sync' paragraph. These should either be the same value (fetch and URL update happen together) or the spec should explicitly state why they differ (e.g., URL updates are more expensive due to history API calls). I'd recommend 300ms for both — 200ms is too aggressive on slow 3G connections and will fire requests that get immediately aborted.

The spec references a `scripts/check-bundle-size.js` that parses meta.json but never specifies the implementation. It's 15 lines of code — include it in the spec: read meta.json, sum outputs[*].bytes, gzip via zlib.gzipSync, compare to 80KB threshold, exit(1) if over. Without this, someone will write it wrong (e.g., checking uncompressed size, or checking a single file instead of total output).

objections.description: Bottom sheets lack URL representation and deep-linkability. The specification describes bottom sheets as overlays invoked from contextual buttons but assigns them no route state. This prevents users from sharing specific sheet states (e.g., a specific action form), causes complete state loss on refresh, and violates the principle that primary actions should be addressable. Additionally, without URL state, the hardware back button (Android) and browser back button cannot distinguish between 'close sheet' and 'navigate away', likely causing accidental navigation away from the parent screen when the user intended to merely dismiss the overlay.
objections.suggested_fix: Assign bottom sheets explicit hash routes (e.g., #/doc/:id?sheet=edit or #/doc/:id/edit). Implement sheet-specific routes in the Router that render the sheet as a modal overlay while keeping the parent route visible in the background. On back navigation, close the sheet rather than navigating away from the parent. If sheets must remain transient (non-shareable), document this as an explicit architectural debt with a recovery strategy: 'Sheets opened via user interaction are not URL-persistent; refreshing the page returns to the underlying screen without the sheet open.'

objections.description: No error boundary protects the Shell infrastructure. The architecture diagram shows ErrorBoundary wrapping individual tab screens (Home, Search, Library), but the Shell contains critical chrome components: ConnectivityBanner (active polling, network state), ThemeToggle (context consumer), SkipLink, and TabBar. If an error occurs in ConnectivityBanner (e.g., unhandled promise rejection in health check) or ThemeToggle (context corruption), the entire application crashes with no graceful fallback, violating resilient UX architecture. A crash in the header currently bricks the entire SPA.
objections.suggested_fix: Introduce a nested ErrorBoundary strategy: wrap the Shell's chrome (header, connectivity banner, tab bar) in a dedicated 'ChromeErrorBoundary' that, on failure, renders a minimal safe-mode fallback (e.g., 'Critical navigation unavailable [Reload]'). Ensure the main content area ErrorBoundary is independent so that a crash in the header does not prevent screen rendering, and vice versa. Alternatively, wrap ConnectivityBanner and ThemeToggle inside individual error boundaries within the Shell layout.

objections.location: Section 3 (Navigation), Tab bar hiding on Detail routes
objections.description: The specification mandates 'Tab bar is HIDDEN on Detail routes: display:none' without layout transition or reserved space strategy. On mobile devices, the tab bar occupies approximately 56-80px including safe areas. Abruptly setting display:none causes the viewport height to increase, triggering content reflow and Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) values exceeding 0.1. This directly contradicts the Core Web Vitals target (CLS < 0.1) specified in Section 1. The sudden disappearance of primary navigation chrome without animation also creates cognitive discontinuity for users navigating from list to detail views.
objections.suggested_fix: Replace display:none with a visual transition: use transform: translateY(100%) with opacity fade over 200-300ms (cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)) to slide the tab bar out of view. Maintain the document space reservation using visibility:hidden or height preservation during the transition to prevent layout shift. Only remove from layout flow after transition completion. Ensure the main content area has persistent padding-bottom equal to tab bar height + safe area so content does not jump when the bar hides.

objections.description: RouteAnnouncer uses aria-live='assertive' which immediately interrupts screen reader speech. For standard navigation between tabs and drill-down views, 'assertive' is overly aggressive and can cut off the announcement of focused elements or page titles. WCAG 2.1 guidelines recommend 'polite' for navigation updates unless the user requires immediate interruption for critical alerts (e.g., form errors, session timeout).
objections.suggested_fix: Change RouteAnnouncer default to aria-live='polite' with aria-atomic='true'. Reserve 'assertive' exclusively for error states or emergency notifications. Implement tiered announcements: polite for standard route changes (Home → Search), assertive only for blocking error screens or authentication timeouts.

objections.description: Scroll restoration fires on requestAnimationFrame immediately after render, but Detail screens fetch data asynchronously (timeline content). When restoration runs, the content height reflects skeleton placeholders, not final content. As data hydrates and DOM height increases, the restored scroll position becomes meaningless (pointing to wrong content) or scrolls to an arbitrary offset. The current LRU Map stores pixel offsets without accounting for content stability or anchor IDs.
objections.suggested_fix: Defer scroll restoration until after data hydration completes. Implement a content stability check (data fetched AND images loaded AND layout settled) before applying saved scroll positions. Alternatively, abandon pixel-based restoration for Detail views in favor of ID-based restoration: store the ID of the topmost visible item before navigation, then scrollIntoView() that item on return. This is resilient to content height changes.

suggestions.description: Explicitly specify bidirectional binding between SearchComposer input and URL query parameters. Clarify that the input must initialize from URL on mount (including browser back/forward navigation) and that the component must flush pending debounced updates before unmounting to prevent desynchronization where the URL shows 'q=hello' but the input field is empty.

Add explicit aria-label to screen root containers (the tabindex='-1' divs) so screen readers announce the region context when focus moves there on route change. Example: <div tabindex='-1' aria-label='Search results' role='main'>.

objections.location: Section 2 (tokens.css reference) + Section 0 (design token system deliverable)
objections.description: Design token system is referenced throughout but actual token values are never defined. tokens.css is listed as a deliverable but the spec contains zero color values, spacing scale, or typography scale. Cannot implement premium/confident aesthetic without concrete token definitions. References like --accent-default, --surface-0, --text-tertiary, --font-size-lg, --space-4 appear without any actual CSS custom property values.
objections.suggested_fix: Add Section 6: Design Token System with complete CSS custom property definitions including: color palette (dark/light theme pairs with hex values), spacing scale (0-12 or similar), typography scale (xs through 2xl with line-height), motion tokens (duration-fast/normal/slow, easing curves). Example: --accent-default: #8B5CF6; --surface-0: #0F0F11; --text-primary: #FAFAFA; --space-4: 1rem; --font-size-lg: 1.125rem; --duration-fast: 150ms;

objections.location: Section 3 (Tab bar visual states) + Section 4 (Component architecture)
objections.description: Color contrast ratios not specified for any interactive elements. WCAG 2.1 AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text, 3:1 for UI components. Spec mentions --text-tertiary for inactive tabs but provides no contrast guarantee. Premium aesthetic cannot ship with accessibility violations.
objections.suggested_fix: Add contrast ratio requirements to token definitions. Document minimum contrast ratios for each text variant against its background. Example: --text-tertiary on --surface-1 must achieve 4.5:1 minimum. Include automated contrast check in CI using tokens.css values.

objections.description: Motion system is listed as a deliverable but no motion tokens are defined. Spec bans ease/linear timing but doesn't specify what easing curves TO use. No duration scale, no easing function definitions, no animation naming convention.
objections.suggested_fix: Add motion token section: --duration-fast: 150ms, --duration-normal: 250ms, --duration-slow: 400ms; --ease-out-expo: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); --ease-in-out-quad: cubic-bezier(0.45, 0, 0.55, 1). Document which durations apply to which interaction types (hover, tap, navigation, sheet).

objections.description: Font weights defined (regular, medium, semibold) but no font size scale beyond xs (12px) and lg mentions. Premium aesthetic requires complete type scale with consistent ratios. No line-height specifications which directly impacts readability and visual hierarchy.
objections.suggested_fix: Define complete typography scale: --font-size-xs: 0.75rem (12px, line-height 1.4), --font-size-sm: 0.875rem (14px, lh 1.4), --font-size-base: 1rem (16px, lh 1.5), --font-size-lg: 1.125rem (18px, lh 1.5), --font-size-xl: 1.25rem (20px, lh 1.4), --font-size-2xl: 1.5rem (24px, lh 1.3). Specify which weights apply to which sizes.

objections.description: Icon stroke widths described as 'size-specific' but no actual values provided. Phosphor-style icons typically use 1.5px or 2px strokes. Inconsistent stroke widths across icon set will break visual hierarchy.
objections.suggested_fix: Standardize icon stroke width: 1.5px for 16px icons, 2px for 24px icons. Document in icons/README.md. Ensure all SVG icons use stroke='currentColor' and consistent viewBox dimensions.

suggestions.description: Add visual regression testing to migration checklist. Before Step M5 (_legacy removal), run screenshot comparison between Alpine and React implementations for all three key screens to catch visual drift.

Add visual hierarchy documentation to each screen component. Specify which element gets visual priority (largest, highest contrast, top position) for Home, Search, and Detail screens to ensure consistent premium aesthetic.
Specify sheet elevation using shadow tokens rather than opacity alone. Premium aesthetic requires subtle depth: --shadow-sheet: 0 -4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.24) for dark mode, adjusted for light mode.

objections.description: Artifact is truncated mid-component tree at the Library tab panel. Sections 5-8 (Design Token System, Core Layout Shell, Key Screens, Motion System, Dark/Light Theme Switching) are completely missing. This is Round 5 and the spec is incomplete.
objections.suggested_fix: Provide complete artifact with all sections. The deliverables list requires: design token system, core layout shell, at least 3 key screens, motion system, dark/light theme switching. None of these are present in this truncated artifact.

objections.description: Spec relies heavily on the `inert` attribute for accessibility (modal focus trapping, tab panel visibility). Browser support floor is Firefox 90+, but inert is only supported from Firefox 97+. Safari 15.0 doesn't support inert (added in 15.1). This creates a gap for the declared support matrix.
objections.suggested_fix: Either: (1) Add a polyfill like `wicg-inert` to package.json and load it in main.jsx before any UI renders, or (2) Revise browser support floor to Safari 15.1+ and Firefox 97+, or (3) Document the fallback behavior when inert is unavailable (aria-hidden + tabindex=-1 on all focusable descendants).

objections.description: Component architecture mentions 'React.memo — see memo pattern below' multiple times but the memo comparator implementation is never shown. Without explicit comparators, React.memo uses shallow equality which often fails for objects/arrays passed as props, negating the performance benefit.
objections.suggested_fix: Define the memo comparator pattern explicitly. Example for TimelineItem: React.memo(TimelineItem, (prev, next) => prev.id === next.id && prev.status === next.status && prev.timestamp === next.timestamp). Or use a generic deep comparator utility for objects.

objections.description: CI step 'parses meta.json, fails build if dist/main.js gzipped > 80KB' is described but scripts/check-bundle-size.js implementation is not provided. The budget breakdown estimates CSS at ~4KB but CSS is loaded separately — this conflates JS budget with total transfer size.
objections.suggested_fix: Provide the check-bundle-size.js implementation. It should use esbuild's metafile format, calculate gzip size using zlib, and exit with non-zero code if exceeded. Separate JS budget (80KB) from total transfer budget in documentation.

objections.description: Banned APIs list (AbortController.timeout, Array.at, Object.hasOwn, structuredClone, crypto.randomUUID) is documented but there's no automated enforcement. Developers will accidentally use these.
objections.suggested_fix: Add eslint-plugin-escompat or configure ESLint with custom rule to ban these APIs. Alternatively, add a babel plugin that transforms them to polyfills. Document which approach is chosen.

objections.description: Scroll restoration uses an in-memory LRU Map but doesn't specify the implementation. Rolling your own LRU has edge cases (capacity boundary behavior, delete vs pop).
objections.suggested_fix: Either use a proven LRU library (tiny-lru is ~1KB) or provide the exact implementation in utils.js. The spec should show the code, not just describe it.

objections.description: icons/ directory has .jsx files but also an index.js barrel file. Mixed extensions (.jsx vs .js) is inconsistent. esbuild handles both, but this affects editor tooling and grep-ability.
objections.suggested_fix: Standardize: either all icon files are .jsx (since they contain JSX), or rename the barrel to index.jsx for consistency.

suggestions.description: Add a Step M0: Create a git branch named 'frontend-react-migration' before any changes. This allows atomic PR review and easy git revert --no-commit to compare before/after.

Document the exact terminal commands for dev workflow: 'Terminal 1: cd src/frontend/web && npm run dev' and 'Terminal 2: python src/backend/apps/web_server.py'. New developers shouldn't have to infer this.

The 150ms debounce is good, but also consider: (1) Don't announce if the new route is the same as current (hash changes without actual navigation), (2) Clear the live region content after announcement to prevent re-reading on screen reader focus.

orchestrator_response.content:
Production Frontend Implementation Spec — v6 (Convergence Round 6) ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 0. PRE-MIGRATION AUDIT & BACKEND ENDPOINT MAPPING ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Before any frontend code is written, execute this checklist: Step M0: Feature inventory & branch setup. - Create git branch: `git checkout -b frontend-react-migration` - Run the existing Alpine/Tailwind index.html in a browser. - Screenshot every visible screen state and list all interactive features. - Save screenshots to `_legacy/screenshots/` as the regression checklist. - This 10-minute inventory prevents 10 hours of "didn't the old version do X?" Step 1: Audit web_server.py routes. - List every route handler in web_server.py. - Map each existing endpoint to the api.js fetch function it will serve. - If backend uses /api/v1/search, api.js calls /api/v1/search — the frontend adapts to the backend, never the reverse. - Document the exact mapping in a ROUTES.md file at repo root. Step 2: Confirm static-file serving behavior. - Verify web_server.py serves index.html for GET / with no path-based routing for sub-pages. - Confirm it serves static assets from src/frontend/web/ (CSS, JS, fonts, images). - If web_server.py has catch-all or path-based HTML serving, document it — this affects routing strategy. Step 3: Verify API health endpoint exists. - If GET /api/health (or equivalent) exists, map it. If not, flag for backend team to add. - api.js uses this on Shell mount to display connectivity warnings (non-blocking — see Section 7). Step 4: Runtime 404 detection — distinguish dev errors from data 404s. - api.js must log a clear console.error with the endpoint path when it receives a 404. - API-route 404 message: "Endpoint not found: GET /api/foo — verify backend route mapping in ROUTES.md" - Resource 404 (e.g., /api/docs/:id where :id doesn't exist): render — no retry button. - Dev-error 404 (endpoint itself missing): render — retryable. - Distinguish by convention: if the route pattern matches a known endpoint template but the specific resource ID fails, it's a resource 404. If the route pattern itself is unknown, it's a dev error. Step 5: Verify Content-Type headers. - Confirm web_server.py serves .js files with Content-Type: application/javascript (not text/plain). - Confirm .css files with Content-Type: text/css. - Confirm .woff2 files with Content-Type: font/woff2. - Some Python HTTP servers (http.server, SimpleHTTPServer) serve incorrect MIME types. Browsers refuse ES module execution with strict MIME type errors — this is a silent, hard-to-diagnose failure. - If MIME types are wrong, fix in web_server.py before proceeding. Migration sequence (big-bang replacement — no feature flags): Step M1: Copy current index.html → _legacy/index-alpine.html (preservation, not coexistence). Step M2: Create new index.html with React mount point, theme script, CSS links. index.html includes two root divs:
Step M2.5: Create package.json in src/frontend/web/ with: dependencies: react@18, react-dom@18 devDependencies: esbuild@0.20+, eslint, eslint-plugin-react-hooks, eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y scripts: "build": "esbuild src/main.jsx --bundle --format=esm --outdir=dist --minify --target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90 --metafile=meta.json --jsx=automatic" "dev": "esbuild src/main.jsx --bundle --format=esm --outdir=dist --target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90 --jsx=automatic --sourcemap=inline --watch" "check-budget": "node scripts/check-bundle-size.js" "lint": "eslint src/" All paths in package.json scripts are relative to src/frontend/web/ (where package.json lives). Run npm install. Commit package.json and package-lock.json. Add node_modules/ to .gitignore. Add dist/ to .gitignore. Step M3: Run `cd src/frontend/web && npm run build` → dist/main.js. web_server.py serves new index.html at / unchanged. Step M3.5: Verify MIME types (Step 5 above) by loading dist/main.js in browser — check DevTools Network tab for Content-Type and console for MIME errors. Step M4: Smoke-test all API routes from new frontend against running backend. Step M4.5: Visual regression — screenshot all 3 key screens and compare against _legacy/screenshots/. Step M5: Remove _legacy/ after 2 weeks of production stability. Rollback: revert index.html to _legacy/index-alpine.html. Zero backend changes required. Dev workflow (exact terminal commands): Terminal 1: cd src/frontend/web && npm run dev Terminal 2: python src/backend/apps/web_server.py esbuild watch rebuilds on file change. web_server.py serves the whole web/ directory. API requests go to web_server.py directly (same origin, no CORS). No proxy needed. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 1. BUILD SYSTEM & MODULE STRATEGY ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Build tool: esbuild (single dependency, zero-config). Decision: esbuild bundles React into the output. No vendor/ directory. No import maps. Rationale: import maps require Safari 16.4+ / Firefox 108+ minimum. Bundling React eliminates the compatibility risk entirely and simplifies the build to a single command. The bundle size difference is zero since React ends up in the output either way. Production command (run from src/frontend/web/): npx esbuild src/main.jsx \ --bundle \ --format=esm \ --outdir=dist \ --minify \ --target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90 \ --metafile=meta.json \ --jsx=automatic Development command (run from src/frontend/web/): npx esbuild src/main.jsx \ --bundle \ --format=esm \ --outdir=dist \ --target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90 \ --jsx=automatic \ --sourcemap=inline \ --watch NOTE: If running from repo root, prefix all commands with `cd src/frontend/web &&`. All paths in this spec are relative to src/frontend/web/ unless explicitly stated otherwise. --jsx=automatic rationale: enables React 18's automatic JSX runtime. Components do NOT need `import React from 'react'` at the top. esbuild injects jsx-runtime imports automatically. Without this flag, any component missing the React import silently fails at runtime. Target rationale: using explicit browser targets instead of es2020 lets esbuild make optimal per-browser syntax decisions. This is self-documenting of the actual support matrix. IMPORTANT: esbuild --target downlevels SYNTAX only, not runtime APIs. Banned APIs (not available on browser floor): - AbortController.timeout() — Safari 16+ only - Array.prototype.at() — Safari 15.4+ only (borderline, avoid) - Object.hasOwn() — Safari 15.4+ only - structuredClone() — Safari 15.4+ only - crypto.randomUUID() — Safari 15.4+ only Enforcement: Add ESLint no-restricted-properties / no-restricted-globals rules: "no-restricted-properties": ["error", { "object": "Object", "property": "hasOwn", "message": "Use Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call() — Object.hasOwn unsupported on Safari <15.4" }, { "property": "at", "message": "Use bracket notation — Array.at() unsupported on Safari <15.4" } ], "no-restricted-globals": ["error", { "name": "structuredClone", "message": "Use JSON parse/stringify or manual clone — structuredClone unsupported on Safari <15.4" } ] CSS strategy: tokens.css and animations.css are NOT imported by JS. They are standalone references in index.html, loaded directly by the browser. esbuild does not process them. This is intentional — global design tokens and keyframes are authored CSS, not component CSS. No --splitting in v1 (splitting requires import maps for shared chunks in browsers; we just eliminated import maps). Single entry bundle. Code-split in v2 via dynamic import() if bundle exceeds budget. Bundle budget enforcement: CI step: `npm run lint && npm run build && npm run check-budget` Budget: dist/main.js gzipped ≤ 80KB. Budget breakdown (estimated): React 18 + ReactDOM: ~42KB gzipped App components: ~15KB gzipped Icons (tree-shaken): ~3KB gzipped Total JS: ~60KB gzipped (20KB headroom to 80KB cap) Separate from JS budget: CSS (tokens + anim): ~4KB gzipped (loaded via , not in JS bundle) Fonts (subsetted): ≤25KB gzipped total (see font budget below) Total transfer: ~89KB gzipped first load scripts/check-bundle-size.js implementation: const fs = require('fs'); const zlib = require('zlib'); const meta = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('meta.json', 'utf8')); const BUDGET = 80 * 1024; // 80KB let totalGzip = 0; for (const [file, info] of Object.entries(meta.outputs)) { if (file.endsWith('.js')) { const raw = fs.readFileSync(file); const gzipped = zlib.gzipSync(raw); console.log(` ${file}: ${(raw.length / 1024).toFixed(1)}KB raw, ${(gzipped.length / 1024).toFixed(1)}KB gzip`); totalGzip += gzipped.length; } } console.log(`\nTotal JS gzipped: ${(totalGzip / 1024).toFixed(1)}KB / ${(BUDGET / 1024).toFixed(0)}KB budget`); if (totalGzip > BUDGET) { console.error('BUDGET EXCEEDED'); process.exit(1); } Preact escape hatch: if bundle exceeds 80KB, switch via esbuild alias: --alias:react=preact/compat --alias:react-dom=preact/compat Preact compat is ~4KB vs React's 42KB. Document this as v1.1 option, not default. Font budget: Inter in 2 weights for v1: Regular (400) and Semibold (600). Medium (500) dropped. Subset to Basic Latin + Latin-1 Supplement + common punctuation: U+0000-00FF, U+2010-2027. Subsetting command: pyftsubset inter-regular.woff2 --unicodes='U+0000-00FF,U+2010-2027' --flavor=woff2 Target per-weight: ≤15KB uncompressed, ≤7KB gzipped. Total font budget: ≤25KB gzipped for both weights. If subsetting tooling is unavailable, document as pre-launch blocker. Medium weight faked via: font-weight: 500 with font-synthesis: weight (browser synthesizes). v1.1: add Medium weight file if synthesized weight is visually unacceptable. Linting (non-negotiable for v1): devDependencies: eslint, eslint-plugin-react-hooks, eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y .eslintrc.json: { "plugins": ["react-hooks", "jsx-a11y"], "extends": ["plugin:jsx-a11y/recommended"], "rules": { "react-hooks/rules-of-hooks": "error", "react-hooks/exhaustive-deps": "warn", "no-restricted-properties": ["error", ...banned API rules above] } } CI runs `npm run lint` before build. This catches stale closures, missing dependencies, and accessibility violations at build time. Core Web Vitals targets (non-negotiable): LCP: < 2.5s on 3G throttled FID / INP: < 100ms CLS: < 0.1 Implementation requirements to hit these: - index.html inlines critical CSS for above-fold content (shell chrome, skeleton placeholders). - tokens.css and animations.css loaded via (not @import — avoids waterfall). - Fonts loaded via with font-display: swap. - All above-fold layout uses explicit dimensions (no layout shift from late-loading content). - Skeleton placeholders match final content dimensions to prevent CLS on data arrival. - Main content area uses persistent padding-bottom for tab bar space (see Section 3 — CLS prevention). Source code lives in src/frontend/web/src/ (JSX, full React idioms). Built output lands in src/frontend/web/dist/ (static ES module, minified, tree-shaken). index.html references dist/main.js via

unresolved_objections.location: Section 4 — COMPONENT ARCHITECTURE MAP (bottom of artifact)
unresolved_objections.description: The artifact is truncated mid-sentence inside the Library tab panel definition. Sections 5 through the end (presumably covering: detail screen tree, api.js implementation, useFetch hook, design token values, animation/motion system, dark/light theme token definitions, skeleton dimension specs, accessibility audit checklist, and the remaining 3 key screens) are entirely missing. This is not a minor omission — the convergence criteria explicitly require 'at least 3 key screens', 'motion system', 'dark/light theme switching', and 'design token system' to be fully specified before any agent can APPROVE. The spec currently delivers ~60% of the required deliverables.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Complete the artifact in full. At minimum, the following sections must exist with implementation-ready detail: (1) Remainder of Section 4 — Library tab panel, Detail screen component tree, BottomSheet tree. (2) Section 5 — Design Token System with actual HSL values for every token in both dark and light modes. (3) Section 6 — Motion System with exact keyframe definitions, timing functions (cubic-bezier values), durations, and reduced-motion overrides. (4) Section 7 — api.js specification (timeout handling, retry logic, response parsing, error classification). (5) Section 8 — useFetch hook contract (loading/error/data states, AbortController lifecycle, stale-while-revalidate or not). (6) Section 9 — Screen-by-screen layout specifications for Home, Search/Detail, and Library with exact spacing, responsive breakpoint behaviors, and skeleton-to-content transition rules. (7) Section 10 — Accessibility compliance matrix (WCAG 2.1 AA checklist mapped to components).

unresolved_objections.description: The spec uses `inert` as a JSX attribute on React elements (e.g., `inert={activeTab !== 'home'}`). React 18 does NOT forward the `inert` attribute to the DOM — it silently drops unknown boolean HTML attributes that aren't in its allowlist. This was only fixed in React 19. On React 18, every inactive tab panel will remain keyboard-navigable and screen-reader-visible, completely breaking the lazy-mount isolation model. This is a correctness bug that will ship silently.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Use a ref-based approach: `useEffect(() => { if (ref.current) { if (isInactive) ref.current.setAttribute('inert', ''); else ref.current.removeAttribute('inert'); } }, [isInactive])`. Apply the same pattern to the bottom sheet's inert management of <main> and <TabBar>. Document this as a known React 18 limitation in the spec with the exact workaround code. Alternatively, add a thin `<InertContainer active={boolean}>` wrapper component that encapsulates the ref logic.

unresolved_objections.description: The build command references `src/main.jsx` as the entrypoint in package.json scripts, but the actual file lives at `src/frontend/web/src/main.jsx`. The esbuild CLI command in Section 1 uses the full path, but the npm script in M2.5 uses a relative path. Since package.json lives in `src/frontend/web/`, the correct entrypoint is `src/main.jsx` relative to that directory — BUT the `--outdir=dist` is also relative, which is correct. However, the production command in Section 1 uses absolute repo-relative paths (`src/frontend/web/src/main.jsx`, `src/frontend/web/dist`). These two invocation patterns are inconsistent and will cause CI/CD confusion about working directory expectations.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Standardize: package.json scripts use paths relative to `src/frontend/web/` (where package.json lives). The Section 1 'production command' and 'development command' blocks should explicitly state `cd src/frontend/web && npx esbuild src/main.jsx ...` or simply reference `npm run build` from that directory. Remove the absolute-path versions or label them clearly as 'if running from repo root'.

unresolved_objections.description: React's useState does NOT trigger re-renders when you mutate a Set in place. The implementation note says `add tab to mountedTabs Set if not present, then toggle visibility` but doesn't specify the immutable update pattern. A naive `mountedTabs.add('search')` won't re-render. This will manifest as: user taps Search tab, nothing mounts, no error thrown. Silent failure.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Specify the exact state update pattern: `setMountedTabs(prev => prev.has(tabId) ? prev : new Set([...prev, tabId]))`. This creates a new Set reference, triggering the re-render. Alternatively, use an object `{ home: true, search: false, library: false }` which is more idiomatic for React state and avoids the Set serialization footgun entirely.

unresolved_objections.description: The spec mentions 'visibility-aware 30s poll' for StatusCard and '15s retry interval' for ConnectivityBanner but never defines the visibility-awareness implementation. Is this Page Visibility API? IntersectionObserver? Both? What happens when the tab is backgrounded — does polling stop? What happens on tab-panel hide (display:none + inert) — does a StatusCard on the Home tab keep polling when the user switches to Search? display:none elements are NOT reported as invisible by IntersectionObserver (they're removed from the rendering tree entirely, so IO never fires for them). This needs an explicit implementation contract.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Define: (1) Use Page Visibility API (`document.visibilitychange`) to pause ALL polling when browser tab is backgrounded. (2) Use a custom `useTabActive(tabId)` hook that reads from Shell's active tab state to pause polling when the React tab panel is hidden. IntersectionObserver is the wrong tool here because display:none bypasses it. (3) On resume: do NOT immediately fire — wait for the next interval tick. This prevents thundering herd on tab-foreground.

unresolved_objections.description: The spec specifies Inter in 3 weights (regular, medium, semibold) as .woff2 files. Inter Regular is ~96KB, Medium ~97KB, Semibold ~98KB uncompressed (~38-40KB each gzipped). That's ~115KB gzipped for fonts alone. The CSS budget is listed as ~4KB but the font budget is completely absent. Fonts are the single largest asset category and will dominate LCP on 3G. The spec's LCP target of <2.5s on 3G is unachievable with 3 unconstrained font files.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Subset the fonts. Inter full Latin is ~96KB per weight; subsetting to Basic Latin + Latin-1 Supplement (U+0000-00FF) brings each weight to ~15-18KB (~6-7KB gzipped). Add a font subsetting step to the build: `pyftsubset inter-regular.woff2 --unicodes='U+0000-00FF,U+2010-2027' --flavor=woff2`. Add font budget to the budget table: ≤25KB gzipped total for all 3 weights. If subsetting tooling is out of scope for v1, reduce to 2 weights (regular + semibold — drop medium) and use font-weight numeric values to fake medium via font-synthesis.

unresolved_objections.location: Section 3 — Bottom sheet, focus trap + portal
unresolved_objections.description: The spec says bottom sheet uses a portal to document.body, and the focus trap is inside the sheet. But the `inert` management strategy says 'TabBar AND <main> receive inert'. If the portal renders OUTSIDE <main> (which it does — it's on document.body), and you're setting inert on <main>, the portal content is NOT inside an inert subtree, which is correct. But the legacy fallback says 'add aria-hidden to all direct children of <body> except the portal root'. This requires knowing the portal root's DOM identity at the point where you're iterating body children. The spec doesn't define the portal root element's ID or how the inert-management code identifies it.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix:
Define: index.html includes `
` as a sibling to `
`. Portal component renders into #portal-root. Inert management iterates `document.body.children`, skips `#portal-root`, sets aria-hidden on everything else. This is deterministic and doesn't require runtime portal detection.

unresolved_objections.description: The spec says tab switches use `history.replaceState()` to avoid history pollution. But with hash-based routing, the actual navigation is changing `location.hash`. Directly setting `location.hash = '#/search'` triggers a hashchange event AND pushes a history entry. You MUST use `history.replaceState(null, '', '#/search')` explicitly — setting location.hash is NOT equivalent. The spec should call this out explicitly to prevent the most common implementation mistake.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Add a boxed warning in the navigation.jsx contract: 'NEVER set location.hash directly for tab switches. Always use history.replaceState(state, "", "#" + path). Setting location.hash pushes to history stack, defeating the replace semantics.' Add this as a code comment in the navigate/replace function.

unresolved_objections.description: Bottom sheets lack URL representation and deep-linkability. The specification describes bottom sheets as overlays invoked from contextual buttons but assigns them no route state. This prevents users from sharing specific sheet states (e.g., a specific action form), causes complete state loss on refresh, and violates the principle that primary actions should be addressable. Additionally, without URL state, the hardware back button (Android) and browser back button cannot distinguish between 'close sheet' and 'navigate away', likely causing accidental navigation away from the parent screen when the user intended to merely dismiss the overlay.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Assign bottom sheets explicit hash routes (e.g., #/doc/:id?sheet=edit or #/doc/:id/edit). Implement sheet-specific routes in the Router that render the sheet as a modal overlay while keeping the parent route visible in the background. On back navigation, close the sheet rather than navigating away from the parent. If sheets must remain transient (non-shareable), document this as an explicit architectural debt with a recovery strategy: 'Sheets opened via user interaction are not URL-persistent; refreshing the page returns to the underlying screen without the sheet open.'

unresolved_objections.description: No error boundary protects the Shell infrastructure. The architecture diagram shows ErrorBoundary wrapping individual tab screens (Home, Search, Library), but the Shell contains critical chrome components: ConnectivityBanner (active polling, network state), ThemeToggle (context consumer), SkipLink, and TabBar. If an error occurs in ConnectivityBanner (e.g., unhandled promise rejection in health check) or ThemeToggle (context corruption), the entire application crashes with no graceful fallback, violating resilient UX architecture. A crash in the header currently bricks the entire SPA.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Introduce a nested ErrorBoundary strategy: wrap the Shell's chrome (header, connectivity banner, tab bar) in a dedicated 'ChromeErrorBoundary' that, on failure, renders a minimal safe-mode fallback (e.g., 'Critical navigation unavailable [Reload]'). Ensure the main content area ErrorBoundary is independent so that a crash in the header does not prevent screen rendering, and vice versa. Alternatively, wrap ConnectivityBanner and ThemeToggle inside individual error boundaries within the Shell layout.

unresolved_objections.location: Section 3 (Navigation), Tab bar hiding on Detail routes
unresolved_objections.description: The specification mandates 'Tab bar is HIDDEN on Detail routes: display:none' without layout transition or reserved space strategy. On mobile devices, the tab bar occupies approximately 56-80px including safe areas. Abruptly setting display:none causes the viewport height to increase, triggering content reflow and Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) values exceeding 0.1. This directly contradicts the Core Web Vitals target (CLS < 0.1) specified in Section 1. The sudden disappearance of primary navigation chrome without animation also creates cognitive discontinuity for users navigating from list to detail views.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Replace display:none with a visual transition: use transform: translateY(100%) with opacity fade over 200-300ms (cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)) to slide the tab bar out of view. Maintain the document space reservation using visibility:hidden or height preservation during the transition to prevent layout shift. Only remove from layout flow after transition completion. Ensure the main content area has persistent padding-bottom equal to tab bar height + safe area so content does not jump when the bar hides.

unresolved_objections.description: RouteAnnouncer uses aria-live='assertive' which immediately interrupts screen reader speech. For standard navigation between tabs and drill-down views, 'assertive' is overly aggressive and can cut off the announcement of focused elements or page titles. WCAG 2.1 guidelines recommend 'polite' for navigation updates unless the user requires immediate interruption for critical alerts (e.g., form errors, session timeout).
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Change RouteAnnouncer default to aria-live='polite' with aria-atomic='true'. Reserve 'assertive' exclusively for error states or emergency notifications. Implement tiered announcements: polite for standard route changes (Home → Search), assertive only for blocking error screens or authentication timeouts.

unresolved_objections.description: Scroll restoration fires on requestAnimationFrame immediately after render, but Detail screens fetch data asynchronously (timeline content). When restoration runs, the content height reflects skeleton placeholders, not final content. As data hydrates and DOM height increases, the restored scroll position becomes meaningless (pointing to wrong content) or scrolls to an arbitrary offset. The current LRU Map stores pixel offsets without accounting for content stability or anchor IDs.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Defer scroll restoration until after data hydration completes. Implement a content stability check (data fetched AND images loaded AND layout settled) before applying saved scroll positions. Alternatively, abandon pixel-based restoration for Detail views in favor of ID-based restoration: store the ID of the topmost visible item before navigation, then scrollIntoView() that item on return. This is resilient to content height changes.

unresolved_objections.location: Section 2 (tokens.css reference) + Section 0 (design token system deliverable)
unresolved_objections.description: Design token system is referenced throughout but actual token values are never defined. tokens.css is listed as a deliverable but the spec contains zero color values, spacing scale, or typography scale. Cannot implement premium/confident aesthetic without concrete token definitions. References like --accent-default, --surface-0, --text-tertiary, --font-size-lg, --space-4 appear without any actual CSS custom property values.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Add Section 6: Design Token System with complete CSS custom property definitions including: color palette (dark/light theme pairs with hex values), spacing scale (0-12 or similar), typography scale (xs through 2xl with line-height), motion tokens (duration-fast/normal/slow, easing curves). Example: --accent-default: #8B5CF6; --surface-0: #0F0F11; --text-primary: #FAFAFA; --space-4: 1rem; --font-size-lg: 1.125rem; --duration-fast: 150ms;

unresolved_objections.location: Section 3 (Tab bar visual states) + Section 4 (Component architecture)
unresolved_objections.description: Color contrast ratios not specified for any interactive elements. WCAG 2.1 AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text, 3:1 for UI components. Spec mentions --text-tertiary for inactive tabs but provides no contrast guarantee. Premium aesthetic cannot ship with accessibility violations.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Add contrast ratio requirements to token definitions. Document minimum contrast ratios for each text variant against its background. Example: --text-tertiary on --surface-1 must achieve 4.5:1 minimum. Include automated contrast check in CI using tokens.css values.

unresolved_objections.description: Motion system is listed as a deliverable but no motion tokens are defined. Spec bans ease/linear timing but doesn't specify what easing curves TO use. No duration scale, no easing function definitions, no animation naming convention.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Add motion token section: --duration-fast: 150ms, --duration-normal: 250ms, --duration-slow: 400ms; --ease-out-expo: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); --ease-in-out-quad: cubic-bezier(0.45, 0, 0.55, 1). Document which durations apply to which interaction types (hover, tap, navigation, sheet).

unresolved_objections.description: Font weights defined (regular, medium, semibold) but no font size scale beyond xs (12px) and lg mentions. Premium aesthetic requires complete type scale with consistent ratios. No line-height specifications which directly impacts readability and visual hierarchy.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Define complete typography scale: --font-size-xs: 0.75rem (12px, line-height 1.4), --font-size-sm: 0.875rem (14px, lh 1.4), --font-size-base: 1rem (16px, lh 1.5), --font-size-lg: 1.125rem (18px, lh 1.5), --font-size-xl: 1.25rem (20px, lh 1.4), --font-size-2xl: 1.5rem (24px, lh 1.3). Specify which weights apply to which sizes.

unresolved_objections.description: Icon stroke widths described as 'size-specific' but no actual values provided. Phosphor-style icons typically use 1.5px or 2px strokes. Inconsistent stroke widths across icon set will break visual hierarchy.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Standardize icon stroke width: 1.5px for 16px icons, 2px for 24px icons. Document in icons/README.md. Ensure all SVG icons use stroke='currentColor' and consistent viewBox dimensions.

unresolved_objections.description: Artifact is truncated mid-component tree at the Library tab panel. Sections 5-8 (Design Token System, Core Layout Shell, Key Screens, Motion System, Dark/Light Theme Switching) are completely missing. This is Round 5 and the spec is incomplete.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Provide complete artifact with all sections. The deliverables list requires: design token system, core layout shell, at least 3 key screens, motion system, dark/light theme switching. None of these are present in this truncated artifact.

unresolved_objections.description: Spec relies heavily on the `inert` attribute for accessibility (modal focus trapping, tab panel visibility). Browser support floor is Firefox 90+, but inert is only supported from Firefox 97+. Safari 15.0 doesn't support inert (added in 15.1). This creates a gap for the declared support matrix.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Either: (1) Add a polyfill like `wicg-inert` to package.json and load it in main.jsx before any UI renders, or (2) Revise browser support floor to Safari 15.1+ and Firefox 97+, or (3) Document the fallback behavior when inert is unavailable (aria-hidden + tabindex=-1 on all focusable descendants).

unresolved_objections.description: Component architecture mentions 'React.memo — see memo pattern below' multiple times but the memo comparator implementation is never shown. Without explicit comparators, React.memo uses shallow equality which often fails for objects/arrays passed as props, negating the performance benefit.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Define the memo comparator pattern explicitly. Example for TimelineItem: React.memo(TimelineItem, (prev, next) => prev.id === next.id && prev.status === next.status && prev.timestamp === next.timestamp). Or use a generic deep comparator utility for objects.

unresolved_objections.description: CI step 'parses meta.json, fails build if dist/main.js gzipped > 80KB' is described but scripts/check-bundle-size.js implementation is not provided. The budget breakdown estimates CSS at ~4KB but CSS is loaded separately — this conflates JS budget with total transfer size.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Provide the check-bundle-size.js implementation. It should use esbuild's metafile format, calculate gzip size using zlib, and exit with non-zero code if exceeded. Separate JS budget (80KB) from total transfer budget in documentation.

unresolved_objections.description: Banned APIs list (AbortController.timeout, Array.at, Object.hasOwn, structuredClone, crypto.randomUUID) is documented but there's no automated enforcement. Developers will accidentally use these.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Add eslint-plugin-escompat or configure ESLint with custom rule to ban these APIs. Alternatively, add a babel plugin that transforms them to polyfills. Document which approach is chosen.

unresolved_objections.description: Scroll restoration uses an in-memory LRU Map but doesn't specify the implementation. Rolling your own LRU has edge cases (capacity boundary behavior, delete vs pop).
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Either use a proven LRU library (tiny-lru is ~1KB) or provide the exact implementation in utils.js. The spec should show the code, not just describe it.

unresolved_objections.description: icons/ directory has .jsx files but also an index.js barrel file. Mixed extensions (.jsx vs .js) is inconsistent. esbuild handles both, but this affects editor tooling and grep-ability.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Standardize: either all icon files are .jsx (since they contain JSX), or rename the barrel to index.jsx for consistency.
